User Story:
As a Admin, I should be able to have acces to handle payroll settings for
 - Pay Period
 - Workweek
 - The last Pay period
 - Over time calculation
 - Lable executions
Description of User Story: This story enables the Admin to configure payroll settings that govern how employee pay is calculated across the facility. The settings include Pay Period configuration (weekly, bi-weekly, semi-monthly, monthly), Workweek definition (which day starts the work week), Last Pay Period reference (the most recent completed pay period), Overtime Calculation rules (daily threshold, weekly threshold, rates), and Label Executions (custom pay labels). These foundational settings affect every timecard and payroll calculation in the system — incorrect configuration leads to inaccurate pay for all employees.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Admin opens Payroll Settings, Then all configuration sections are displayed.
 2. If Pay Period is set to 'Bi-weekly', Then payroll cycles run every two weeks.
  Else if 'Monthly', Then cycles run once per month.
 3. If Workweek start is set to Monday, Then overtime weekly calculation resets every Monday.
 4. If daily overtime threshold is set to 8 hours, Then any day exceeding 8 hours flags as overtime.
 5. If weekly overtime threshold is set to 40 hours, Then hours beyond 40 in a work week are overtime.
 6. After any setting is saved, Then it takes effect for the next pay period.
 7. If settings are changed mid-cycle, Then a warning is shown that changes apply to the next cycle only.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Payroll Settings page shows sections for Pay Period, Workweek, Last Pay Period, Overtime Calculation, and Label Executions
 2. Verify that selecting 'Bi-weekly' as Pay Period and saving applies to future cycles
 3. Verify that setting Workweek start to 'Monday' affects overtime weekly reset
 4. Verify that setting daily OT threshold to 8 hours flags timecards with >8 hour days
 5. Verify that setting weekly OT threshold to 40 hours flags timecards with >40 hour weeks
 6. Verify that setting OT rate to 1.5x calculates correctly (e.g., $20/hr * 1.5 = $30/hr OT)
 7. Verify that updating Last Pay Period changes the reference for next cycle
 8. Verify that custom label executions appear on timecards and reports
 
 Negative:
 9. Verify that setting daily OT threshold to 0 or negative shows validation error
 10. Verify that setting OT rate to 0 shows validation error
 11. Verify that changing settings mid-cycle shows a warning about next-cycle application
 
 UI Scenarios:
 12. Verify that Pay Period shows dropdown with: Weekly, Bi-weekly, Semi-monthly, Monthly
 13. Verify that Workweek shows day selection (Monday through Sunday)
 14. Verify that OT thresholds show numeric input fields
 
 Edge Cases:
 15. Verify that changing from Monthly to Weekly pay period recalculates all pending timecards
 16. Verify that holiday OT rate (2x) applies correctly on designated holidays
 
 Security:
 17. Verify that only Admin role can access Payroll Settings
 18. Verify that all setting changes are logged in an audit trail
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that Payroll Settings page loads within 2 seconds
 2. Verify that saving settings completes within 3 seconds

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to track document expiration with automated alerts.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to track employee document expiration dates and receive automated alerts when documents are approaching or past their expiry. The system monitors all employee documents (licenses, certifications, visas, background checks) and triggers alerts at configurable thresholds (e.g., 90 days, 60 days, 30 days, 7 days before expiry). Alerts are sent via email, in-app notification, or both. This is critical for maintaining facility compliance and avoiding regulatory violations from expired staff credentials.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a document is within the alert threshold, Then an automated alert is generated.
 2. If the threshold is set to 30 days, Then alerts fire 30 days before expiry.
 3. If multiple thresholds are configured (90, 60, 30, 7 days), Then alerts fire at each threshold.
 4. After an alert is generated, Then it appears on the Dashboard and is sent via configured channels (email, in-app).
 5. If the document is renewed, Then pending alerts for the old expiry are cancelled.
 6. If the document has expired, Then an urgent 'Expired' alert is shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that a document expiring in 30 days triggers an alert when threshold is set to 30
 2. Verify that alerts appear in the Dashboard Compliance Alert section
 3. Verify that alerts include employee name, document type, and expiry date
 4. Verify that configuring thresholds at 90/60/30/7 days triggers alerts at each interval
 5. Verify that renewing a document cancels pending alerts
 6. Verify that expired documents show 'Expired' urgent indicator
 
 Negative:
 7. Verify that a document with no expiry date does not trigger alerts
 8. Verify that a document already renewed does not trigger old alerts
 
 UI Scenarios:
 9. Verify that alert threshold configuration shows numeric fields for each interval
 10. Verify that alert channels (email, in-app) can be toggled on/off
 
 Edge Cases:
 11. Verify that 100 documents expiring on the same day triggers 100 separate alerts
 12. Verify that changing the threshold after alerts were sent does not duplicate alerts
 
 Security:
 13. Verify that alerts are only sent to authorized person
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that alert generation runs within 1 minute of the scheduled check
 2. Verify that email alerts are delivered within 5 minutes

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to monitor employee compliance requirements.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to monitor overall employee compliance status across the facility. The compliance dashboard shows which employees meet all requirements (fully compliant), which have expiring items (at risk), and which have expired items (non-compliant). Compliance requirements include licenses, certifications, TB tests, background checks, training completion, and mandatory document submissions. This bird's-eye view enables proactive compliance management and audit readiness.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the compliance monitoring view loads, Then summary counts for Compliant, At Risk, and Non-Compliant are displayed.
 2. If an employee meets all requirements, Then they show as 'Compliant' in green.
  Else if items are expiring soon, Then 'At Risk' in amber.
  Else if items are expired, Then 'Non-Compliant' in red.
 3. If a non-compliant employee is clicked, Then their specific missing or expired requirements are listed.
 4. If filtered by status, Then only matching employees are shown.
 5. After a new requirement is added, Then employees without it are flagged as non-compliant.
 6. If the provider exports, Then a compliance report is generated.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that compliance view shows Compliant, At Risk, and Non-Compliant counts
 2. Verify that each employee shows compliance status with color coding
 3. Verify that clicking a non-compliant employee shows specific expired/missing items
 4. Verify that filtering by 'Non-Compliant' shows only non-compliant employees
 5. Verify that adding a new requirement flags employees without it
 6. Verify that exporting generates a downloadable compliance report
 
 Negative:
 7. Verify that an employee with all items current shows as 'Compliant'
 8. Verify that removing a requirement updates compliance status for affected employees
 
 UI Scenarios:
 9. Verify that green=Compliant, amber=At Risk, red=Non-Compliant color coding is used
 10. Verify that the summary counts update in real-time when compliance changes
 
 Edge Cases:
 11. Verify that 500+ employees display with pagination and sorting
 12. Verify that an employee with 10+ non-compliant items shows all in their detail view
 
 Security:
 13. Verify that compliance data is only visible to authorized HR roles
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the compliance monitoring view loads within 3 seconds
 2. Verify that the compliance report generates within 10 seconds

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to track vacation and PTO.
Description of User Story:
This story allows providers to track employee vacation and PTO in one place. It shows how many days are allocated, used, and remaining for each employee, along with their accrual rate.
The PTO screen displays leave requests as cards with a progress bar (e.g., “7 out of 15 days used”), making it easy to understand leave usage. This helps providers manage leave fairly and maintain proper staffing coverage.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When PTO tracking is opened, Then employee allocations, used days, and remaining balances are shown.
 2. If an employee has used PTO, Then the Leave Quota bar shows the used portion in green and remaining in gray.
  Else if no PTO used, Then the full allocation is shown as remaining.
 3. If accrual is configured, Then balances increase automatically at each accrual period.
 4. If a specific employee is selected, Then their complete PTO history is displayed.
 5. If the provider views the summary, Then aggregate PTO data across all employees is shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that PTO tracking shows allocation, used, and remaining for each employee
 2. Verify that the Leave Quota bar shows '07/15 Days Used' format with visual progress
 3. Verify that PTO accrual increases balance at configured intervals
 4. Verify that clicking an employee shows their complete PTO history
 5. Verify that the facility-wide summary shows aggregate PTO data
 
 Negative:
 6. Verify that a new employee with no PTO yet shows 0 used and full allocation remaining
 7. Verify that exceeding PTO allocation shows a negative balance or warning
 
 UI Scenarios:
 8. Verify that the progress bar uses green for used and gray for remaining
 9. Verify that employees with 0 remaining PTO are highlighted
 
 Edge Cases:
 10. Verify that PTO accrual on a leap year calculates correctly
 11. Verify that transferring unused PTO to next year works when policy allows
 
 Security:
 12. Verify that PTO data is only accessible to authorized roles
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that PTO tracking loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to manage employee onboarding documentation and background checks.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to manage the onboarding documentation and background check process for new employees. The provider can track which onboarding documents have been submitted, review background check results, and ensure all pre-employment requirements are met before activation. This ties into the 3-step onboarding workflow (Pre-Employment Screening, Mandatory Document Collection, Employment Activation) visible in the Figma screens.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the provider views an onboarding employee's documentation, Then all required documents and their status are shown.
 2. If a background check is pending, Then status shows 'Pending'.
  Else if clear, Then 'Clear'.
  Else if flagged, Then 'Flagged' with details.
 3. If all documents are submitted and checks are clear, Then the employee is eligible for activation.
 4. If a background check is flagged, Then the provider reviews and approves or rejects.
 5. After pre-employment screening is approved, Then the step is marked complete and onboarding advances.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that onboarding documentation shows all required documents with status
 2. Verify that background check status shows as Pending, Clear, or Flagged
 3. Verify that all-clear status enables employee activation
 4. Verify that flagged background check shows details for review
 5. Verify that approving screening marks the step complete
 
 Negative:
 6. Verify that activating an employee with pending documents is blocked
 7. Verify that activating with a flagged background check shows a warning
 
 UI Scenarios:
 8. Verify that document status uses checkmarks (submitted) and X marks (missing)
 9. Verify that background check status uses color coding (green=clear, red=flagged, gray=pending)
 
 Edge Cases:
 10. Verify that re-running a background check after a flag is resolved updates the status
 
 Security:
 11. Verify that background check results are only visible to authorized HR roles
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the documentation tab loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As a provider, I should be able to manage timesheets based on patient service hours.
Description of User Story: This story enables the provider to manage employee timesheets that are tied to patient service hours. Unlike standard clock-in/out timesheets, these track hours spent providing specific patient services (therapy, personal care, medication administration). The provider can view, verify, and approve timesheets that link employee work hours to the patients they served and the service types delivered. This is critical for Medicaid billing accuracy and compliance.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When timesheet management is opened, Then timesheets with employee, service hours, patient, and service type are displayed.
 2. If a timesheet has patient service linkage, Then hours are broken down per patient and service type.
 3. If the provider approves a timesheet, Then it forwards to payroll and billing.
 4. If discrepancies are found, Then they are highlighted for review.
 5. After approval, Then hours feed into both payroll calculations and Medicaid billing claims.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that timesheets show employee name, total service hours, patients served, and service types
 2. Verify that hour breakdown shows per-patient and per-service-type detail
 3. Verify that approving a timesheet forwards it to payroll and billing
 4. Verify that discrepancies between scheduled and logged hours are flagged
 5. Verify that approved hours feed into Medicaid billing claims
 
 Negative:
 6. Verify that approving a timesheet with unresolved discrepancies shows a warning
 7. Verify that a timesheet with 0 service hours cannot be approved
 
 UI Scenarios:
 8. Verify that the timesheet breakdown uses a table format with patient rows and service columns
 9. Verify that flagged discrepancies are highlighted in red
 
 Edge Cases:
 10. Verify that an employee serving 20+ patients in a week displays all in the breakdown
 11. Verify that overlapping service times for the same employee are detected
 
 Security:
 12. Verify that patient names in timesheets are only visible to authorized roles (HIPAA)
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that timesheet management loads within 3 seconds

User Story: As a staff member, my timesheets should be automatically generated from my EVV clock in/out records and scheduled shift data, eliminating manual time entry and ensuring accurate billing and payroll records.
Description of User Story: This story enables automatic timesheet generation for staff members based on their Electronic Visit Verification (EVV) clock in/out records and scheduled shift data. Instead of manually entering hours, the system aggregates EVV data and shift schedules to produce completed timesheets. This eliminates manual entry errors, ensures billing accuracy for Medicaid compliance, and reduces administrative burden. The auto-generated timesheet shows the source data (EVV record or schedule) for each time entry.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When EVV clock in/out records exist for a pay period, Then a timesheet is auto-generated from that data.
 2. If no EVV data exists for a scheduled shift, Then the scheduled times are used as default.
 3. If the auto-generated timesheet has discrepancies, Then they are flagged for review.
 4. After the timesheet is generated, Then the staff member can review and submit it.
 5. If the timesheet is approved, Then hours feed into payroll and billing.
 6. If EVV data shows a missed punch (clock-in without clock-out), Then the entry is flagged as 'Missing Punch'.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that timesheets are auto-generated from EVV clock in/out records
 2. Verify that scheduled shift times are used when no EVV data exists
 3. Verify that each entry shows its source (EVV or Schedule)
 4. Verify that the staff member can review and submit the auto-generated timesheet
 5. Verify that approved timesheets feed into payroll and billing
 
 Negative:
 6. Verify that missing EVV clock-out is flagged as 'Missing Punch'
 7. Verify that late clock-in (>15 minutes after shift start) is flagged as discrepancy
 
 UI Scenarios:
 8. Verify that EVV-sourced entries are visually different from schedule-sourced entries
 9. Verify that flagged discrepancies show a warning indicator
 
 Edge Cases:
 10. Verify that a staff member with no EVV records and no scheduled shifts gets an empty timesheet
 11. Verify that daylight saving time transitions calculate hours correctly
 
 Security:
 12. Verify that EVV records cannot be manually altered after auto-generation
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that auto-generation for all staff completes within 5 minutes for a facility of 200 employees
 2. Verify that individual timesheet generation completes within 10 seconds

User Story: As a payroll administrator, the system should automatically calculate overtime (daily/weekly thresholds), shift differentials (night/weekend/holiday rates), and premium pay based on configurable rules, with clear breakdown showing regular hours, overtime hours, and applicable rates for each timesheet entry.
Description of User Story: This story enables the payroll system to automatically calculate complex pay rules including overtime based on configurable daily and weekly thresholds, shift differentials for night shifts, weekends, and holidays, and premium pay categories. The calculation engine applies these rules to each timesheet entry and produces a clear breakdown showing regular hours at base rate, overtime hours at OT rate (e.g., 1.5x), and any shift differentials or premium pay. This automation eliminates manual pay calculations, reduces payroll errors, and ensures compliance with labor laws.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If daily hours exceed the configured threshold, Then daily overtime is calculated.
 2. If weekly hours exceed the configured threshold, Then weekly overtime is calculated.
 3. If the employee worked a night shift, Then the night differential rate applies.
 4. If the employee worked on a weekend, Then the weekend rate applies.
 5. If the employee worked on a holiday, Then premium pay (e.g., 2x) applies.
 6. After all rules are applied, Then a breakdown shows regular hours, OT hours, differentials, and total pay.
 7. If rules are changed, Then future calculations use the updated rules.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that working 10 hours in a day with 8-hour threshold shows 8 regular + 2 overtime
 2. Verify that working 45 hours in a week with 40-hour threshold shows 40 regular + 5 overtime
 3. Verify that night shift hours have the night differential added (e.g., +$2/hr)
 4. Verify that weekend hours have the weekend rate applied
 5. Verify that holiday hours show 2x premium pay
 6. Verify that the breakdown clearly shows regular, OT, differential, and total amounts
 7. Verify that changing OT threshold from 40 to 44 affects future calculations
 
 Negative:
 8. Verify that negative hours are rejected by the engine
 9. Verify that an employee with 0 hours gets $0 calculated
 
 UI Scenarios:
 10. Verify that the pay breakdown is displayed in a clear table format
 11. Verify that overtime hours are highlighted or color-coded
 
 Edge Cases:
 12. Verify that an employee hitting both daily and weekly OT thresholds is not double-counted
 13. Verify that a holiday falling on a weekend applies only one premium (not stacked)
 14. Verify that a 24-hour shift calculates correctly across midnight
 
 Security:
 15. Verify that pay rule configurations are only editable by Admin
 16. Verify that all pay calculations are auditable
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that pay calculations for 200 timesheets complete within 30 seconds
 2. Verify that individual timesheet calculation completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As a supervisor, I should be able to review submitted timesheets with flagged discrepancies (late clock-ins, missing punches, overtime alerts), approve or reject with comments, and route to payroll once approved, with staff receiving notifications of approval status and required corrections.
Description of User Story: This story enables supervisors to review employee timesheets that have been submitted for approval, with automatic flagging of discrepancies. The Figma Shift Change Request screens show a similar review and approval pattern with Approve/Reject buttons and status tracking. Flagged items include late clock-ins (arriving after scheduled start), missing punches (clock-in without clock-out or vice versa), and overtime alerts (hours exceeding thresholds). The supervisor can approve (routing to payroll) or reject with comments (sending back to the employee for corrections). Both approval and rejection trigger notifications to the employee.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a submitted timesheet is reviewed, Then flagged discrepancies are highlighted.
 2. If a late clock-in is detected, Then a 'Late Clock-In' flag with scheduled vs actual time is shown.
 3. If a missing punch is detected, Then a 'Missing Punch' flag is shown.
 4. If overtime exceeds the threshold, Then an 'Overtime Alert' is shown.
 5. If the supervisor clicks Approve, Then the timesheet routes to payroll and the employee is notified.
 6. If the supervisor clicks Reject, Then a comment field appears for the rejection reason.
 7. After rejection, Then the employee receives a notification with required corrections.
 8. If all discrepancies are resolved and approved, Then the timesheet moves to the payroll queue.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that submitted timesheets appear in the supervisor's review queue
 2. Verify that late clock-ins are flagged with scheduled vs actual time (e.g., 'Scheduled: 9:00 AM, Actual: 9:22 AM')
 3. Verify that missing punches are flagged with 'Missing Punch' indicator
 4. Verify that overtime hours are flagged with 'Overtime Alert' and hour count
 5. Verify that clicking Approve routes the timesheet to payroll
 6. Verify that clicking Reject opens a comment field for reason entry
 7. Verify that the employee receives a notification after approval
 8. Verify that the employee receives a notification with corrections after rejection
 
 Negative:
 9. Verify that rejecting without entering a comment shows a validation error
 10. Verify that approving a timesheet with unresolved missing punches shows a warning
 
 UI Scenarios:
 11. Verify that Approve button is blue/green and Reject button is outlined/red
 12. Verify that flagged discrepancies show distinct icons (clock for late, exclamation for missing)
 
 Edge Cases:
 13. Verify that a timesheet with 10+ flagged items displays all clearly
 14. Verify that approving a timesheet already approved by another supervisor is handled
 
 Security:
 15. Verify that supervisors can only review timesheets for their direct reports
 16. Verify that approval actions are logged with supervisor name and timestamp
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the timesheet review queue loads within 2 seconds
 2. Verify that approval/rejection completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As a payroll administrator, I should be able to export approved timesheets in the required format for my payroll vendor (ADP, Gusto, Paychex, QuickBooks) with employee ID mapping, hours breakdown by pay type, and automated file transmission or download for seamless payroll processing.
Description of User Story: This story enables the payroll administrator to export approved timesheets in the specific format required by their payroll vendor. Supported vendors include ADP, Gusto, Paychex, and QuickBooks. The export includes employee ID mapping (system ID to vendor ID), hours breakdown by pay type (regular, overtime, holiday, shift differential), and can be transmitted automatically via API or downloaded as a file. This is the final step in the payroll pipeline — from timesheet approval to payroll processing. Incorrect formatting or missing data causes payroll delays and employee pay issues.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the payroll export section is opened, Then approved timesheets ready for export are listed.
 2. If a vendor is selected (ADP, Gusto, Paychex, QuickBooks), Then the export format adjusts to that vendor.
 3. If Export is clicked, Then a file with employee ID mapping and hours by pay type is generated.
 4. If Download is chosen, Then the file saves in the vendor format.
  Else if Auto-Transmit is chosen, Then the file sends to the vendor via API.
 5. After export, Then a record is logged with date, vendor, file details, and status.
 6. If employee ID mapping is configured, Then system IDs are translated to vendor IDs in the export.
 7. If a mapping is missing for an employee, Then a warning is shown listing unmapped employees.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that the export section shows approved timesheets ready for export
 2. Verify that selecting 'ADP' configures the export for ADP format
 3. Verify that selecting 'Gusto' configures for Gusto format
 4. Verify that the export file contains employee ID mapping (system to vendor)
 5. Verify that hours are broken down by pay type (regular, OT, holiday, differential)
 6. Verify that downloading produces a file in the correct vendor format
 7. Verify that auto-transmission sends the file via API to the vendor
 8. Verify that export history shows date, vendor, file, and status
 
 Negative:
 9. Verify that exporting with no approved timesheets shows 'No timesheets to export'
 10. Verify that a missing employee ID mapping shows a warning with the unmapped employees
 11. Verify that API transmission failure shows an error with retry option
 
 UI Scenarios:
 12. Verify that vendor selection shows dropdown with ADP, Gusto, Paychex, QuickBooks
 13. Verify that export progress is shown during file generation
 
 Edge Cases:
 14. Verify that exporting 500+ timesheets completes without timeout
 15. Verify that exporting the same period twice shows a 'Duplicate export' warning
 
 Security:
 16. Verify that only payroll administrators can access the export function
 17. Verify that exported files containing employee pay data are encrypted
 18. Verify that API credentials for vendor transmission are stored securely
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that export file generation for 200 timesheets completes within 30 seconds
 2. Verify that API transmission completes within 60 seconds
 3. Verify that file download initiates within 5 seconds

User Story:
As a HR Admin, I should be able to view summary cards on header
 - Total Employees
 - Pending Leave Approvals
 - Documents Expiring
 - Payroll Status
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view key workforce metrics as summary cards at the top of the HRMS Dashboard. The Figma screen shows the dashboard header area with four KPI cards: Total Employees (showing the headcount), Pending Leave Approvals (count of leaves awaiting action), Documents Expiring (count of employee documents nearing expiration), and Payroll Status (current payroll cycle status). Each card provides a quick numeric snapshot so the HR Admin can assess overall workforce health at a glance. These cards serve as the primary entry point for the HRMS module and drive the admin's daily priorities — a high number in Pending Leave Approvals or Documents Expiring signals immediate action needed.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the HRMS Dashboard loads, Then four summary cards (Total Employees, Pending Leave Approvals, Documents Expiring, Payroll Status) are displayed in the header.
 2. If active employees exist, Then the Total Employees card shows the current headcount.
  Else if no employees exist, Then the card shows 0.
 3. If pending leave approvals exist, Then the count is displayed on the card.
  Else if no pending approvals, Then the card shows 0.
 4. If documents are expiring within the threshold, Then the Documents Expiring card shows the count.
  Else if no documents are expiring, Then the card shows 0.
 5. If a payroll cycle is active, Then the Payroll Status card shows the current status.
 6. If the HR Admin clicks a summary card, Then they are navigated to the relevant detailed section.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that navigating to HRMS Dashboard displays four summary cards in the header
 2. Verify that Total Employees card shows the correct active employee count
 3. Verify that Pending Leave Approvals card shows the correct count of pending requests
 4. Verify that Documents Expiring card shows documents expiring within 30 days
 5. Verify that Payroll Status card shows current cycle status
 6. Verify that clicking the Total Employees card navigates to the Employees list
 7. Verify that clicking Pending Leave Approvals navigates to Leave Management
 8. Verify that clicking “Documents Expiring” navigates the user to the Documents section with the “Soon” tab pre-selected.
 9. Verify that clicking Payroll Status navigates to the Payroll section
 
 Negative:
 10. Verify that when no employees exist, Total Employees shows 0
 11. Verify that when no pending leaves exist, Pending Leave Approvals shows 0
 12. Verify that when no documents are expiring, Documents Expiring shows 0
 
 UI Scenarios:
 13. Verify that all four cards are displayed horizontally across the header
 14. Verify that each card shows a numeric value and a label
 
 Edge Cases:
 15. Verify that a facility with 5000+ employees displays the count correctly without overflow
 16. Verify that card values update in real-time when a leave is approved from another tab
 
 Security:
 17. Verify that the dashboard only shows data for facilities the HR Admin has access to
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the HRMS Dashboard loads with all four summary cards within 3 seconds
2. Verify that card values are calculated and displayed within 2 seconds

User Story:
As a HR Admin, I should be able see "Leave Requests Awaiting Approval" section with list of pending leave requests with -
 - Record count(On section header)
 - Employee name,
 - Dates : From - To
 - Leave request type
 - Status
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view a Leave Requests Awaiting Approval section on the HRMS Dashboard. The section shows a header with the record count badge, and a list of pending leave requests. Each request card displays the employee name with avatar, date range (From - To), leave request type (PTO, Sick, Annual, Emergency), and current approval status (Supervisor: Pending/Approved, HR: Awaiting/Pending). The Figma Leave Management screen shows requests with PTO badges in orange, date ranges like 'Feb 24 - Feb26 - 3 Days', and dual approval tracking. This section gives the HR Admin immediate visibility into leaves that need their action without navigating away from the dashboard.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Dashboard loads, Then the Leave Requests Awaiting Approval section is displayed with a record count badge.
 2. If pending leave requests exist, Then each request shows employee name, avatar, date range, leave type badge, and approval status.
  Else if no pending requests exist, Then a 'No pending leave requests' message is shown.
 3. If the leave type is PTO, Then an orange badge is shown.
  Else if Sick Leave, Then a different colored badge is shown.
  Else if Annual Leave or Emergency Leave, Then corresponding badges are shown.
 4. If the Supervisor has approved, Then Supervisor status shows 'Approved' and HR status shows 'Pending'.
  Else if Supervisor is still pending, Then Supervisor shows 'Pending' and HR shows 'Awaiting'.
 5. If the HR Admin clicks a request, Then they navigate to the detailed approval view.
 6. After a new leave is submitted, Then the record count and list update automatically.
 7. If the HR Admin approves or rejects a request from the dashboard, Then the request is removed from the queue and the count decreases.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that the Leave Approval Queue section is displayed on the HRMS Dashboard
 2. Verify that the section header shows 'Leave Requests Awaiting Approval' with a record count badge
 3. Verify that each request shows employee name with avatar
 4. Verify that date range is displayed as 'Feb 24 - Feb26 - 3 Days' format
 5. Verify that dual approval tracking shows  HR status separately
 6. Verify that clicking a request navigates to the approval detail view
 7. Verify that the record count updates when a new leave is submitted
 
 Negative:
 8. Verify that when no pending requests exist, 'No pending leave requests' message is shown
 9. Verify that the record count shows '0' when all requests are processed
 
 UI Scenarios:
 10. Verify that employee avatars are displayed next to names
 11. Verify that PTO badge and Sick badge is in different color
 12. Verify that Supervisor 'Pending' shows in orange, HR 'Awaiting' shows in gray
 
 Edge Cases:
 13. Verify that a request approved by another admin disappears from the queue in real-time
 
 Security:
 14. Verify that the queue only shows requests from employees within the admin's facility
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the Leave Approval Queue loads within 2 seconds
2. Verify that the record count updates within 1 second after an action

User Story:
As a HR Admin, I should be able to see "Compliance Alert" section with list of expiring documents
 - Record count(On section header)
 - Employee name
 - Work Visa/ Certification/ Background Check doc
 - Expiry date
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view a Compliance Alert section on the HRMS Dashboard showing employee documents that are approaching expiration. The section displays a header with record count badge and a list of alerts. Each alert shows the employee name, the document type (Work Visa, Certification, or Background Check), and the expiry date. This is critical for regulatory compliance — expired certifications or visas can result in facility violations, fines, or inability to operate. The alerts enable proactive document renewal management.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Dashboard loads, Then the Compliance Alert section is displayed with a record count.
 2. If documents are expiring within the threshold period, Then each alert shows employee name, document type, and expiry date.
  Else if no documents are expiring, Then a 'No compliance alerts' message is shown.
 3. If the document type is Work Visa, Then it is labeled as 'Work Visa'.
  Else if Certification, Then 'Certification'.
  Else if Background Check, Then 'Background Check'.
 4. If the HR Admin clicks an alert, Then they navigate to the employee's document section.
 5. After a document is renewed, Then the alert disappears and the count updates.
 6. If multiple documents for the same employee are expiring, Then each appears as a separate alert.
 7. If a document has already expired (past the date), Then the alert shows with an 'Expired' indicator instead of 'Expiring'.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that the Compliance Alert section is displayed on the Dashboard
 2. Verify that the header shows 'Compliance Alert' with a record count badge
 3. Verify that each alert shows employee name, document type, and expiry date
 4. Verify that document types include Work Visa, Certification, and Background Check
 5. Verify that clicking an alert navigates to the employee's document section
 6. Verify that alerts are sorted by expiry date (soonest first)
 7. Verify that renewing a document removes the alert from the list
 
 Negative:
 8. Verify that no expiring documents shows 'No compliance alerts' message
 9. Verify that the count shows '0' when all documents are current
 
 UI Scenarios:
 10. Verify that expiry dates within 7 days show in red for urgency and "Expiring" indicator
 11. Verify that expiry dates within 30 days show in amber
 12. Verify that already-expired documents show 'Expired' indicator
 
 Edge Cases:
 13. Verify that an employee with 5 expiring documents shows 5 separate alerts
 14. Verify that 100+ compliance alerts load with scrolling
 
 Security:
 15. Verify that alerts only show for employees within the admin's facility scope
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the Compliance Alert section loads within 2 seconds

User Story:
As a HR Admin, I should be able to view "Workforce Movement" section with New Joiners list with
 - New Hire count
 - Exit Count
 - Net Change
 - Name
 - Role
 - Hire date
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view a Workforce Movement section on the Dashboard showing new employee joiners. The section displays summary metrics (New Hire count, Exit Count, Net Change) and a list of new joiners with their name, role, and hire date. This gives HR Admins visibility into workforce growth and turnover trends. The Figma shows this as a dashboard widget with numerical KPIs at the top and a scrollable list below. Net Change (New Hires - Exits) provides an at-a-glance indicator of whether the facility is growing or shrinking its workforce.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Dashboard loads, Then the Workforce Movement section shows New Hire count, Exit Count, and Net Change.
 2. If new hires exist, Then a list displays each joiner's name, role, and hire date.
  Else if no new hires, Then the list shows 'No new hires this period'.
 3. If Net Change is positive (more hires than exits), Then it displays as a positive number.
  Else if negative (more exits), Then it displays as a negative number.
 4. If the HR Admin clicks a joiner, Then they navigate to the employee profile.
 5. After a new employee is onboarded, Then the New Hire count and list update automatically.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Workforce Movement section shows New Hire count, Exit Count, and Net Change
 2. Verify that Net Change = New Hires - Exits
 3. Verify that each new joiner shows name, role and hire date
 4. Verify that joiners are listed with most recent hire first
 5. Verify that clicking a joiner navigates to their profile
 
 Negative:
 6. Verify that no new hires shows 'No new hires this period'
 7. Verify that Net Change shows negative when exits exceed hires
 
 UI Scenarios:
 8. Verify that positive Net Change shows in green, negative in red
 9. Verify that the three KPI values are displayed prominently at the top of the section
 
 Edge Cases:
 10. Verify that 50+ new hires display with scrolling
 11. Verify that an employee hired and then exited in the same period is counted in both
 
 Security:
 12. Verify that workforce data only shows for the admin's authorized facilities
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the Workforce Movement section loads within 2 seconds

User Story:
As a HR Admin, I should be able to view employees leaving with
 - Names
 - Roles
 - Exit dates.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view a list of employees who are leaving or have recently exited the organization within the Workforce Movement section. Each entry shows the employee name, their role, and the exit/separation date. This helps HR Admins track departures, manage knowledge transfer, and ensure proper exit procedures are followed for each leaving employee.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the leaving employees list loads, Then each entry shows name, role, and exit date.
 2. If employees are in separation, Then they are sorted by exit date (nearest first).
  Else if no employees are leaving, Then 'No employees leaving' message is shown.
 3. If the HR Admin clicks a leaving employee, Then they navigate to the exit management details.
 4. After an exit is finalized, Then the employee is removed from the leaving list.
 5. If an employee's exit is cancelled, Then they are removed from the leaving list and restored to active.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that leaving employees list shows name, role, and exit date for each entry
 2. Verify that employees are sorted by exit date (nearest first)
 3. Verify that clicking a leaving employee navigates to exit management
 4. Verify that completed exits are removed from the active leaving list
 
 Negative:
 5. Verify that no leaving employees shows 'No employees leaving' message
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that exit dates within 7 days are highlighted with urgency indicator
 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that an employee whose exit is cancelled disappears from the leaving list
 8. Verify that 30+ leaving employees display with scrolling
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the leaving employees list loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As a HR Admin, I should be able to click "View All" to see complete new hire or exit lists.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to click a 'View All' link on the Workforce Movement section to see the complete list of new hires or exits, beyond the summary shown on the dashboard. The dashboard widget shows a limited preview, and 'View All' expands to the full detailed view with filters, sorting, and all records. This is important when the dashboard preview only shows the top 5-10 entries and the admin needs to see the complete picture.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the HR Admin clicks 'View All' on new hires, Then the complete new hire list is displayed.
 2. When the HR Admin clicks 'View All' on exits, Then the complete exit list is displayed.
 3. If the full list is loaded, Then filter, sort, and search controls are available.
 4. After navigating to the full list, Then the HR Admin can return to the dashboard.
 5. If the full list has more records than can fit on one page, Then pagination is available.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking 'View All' on new hires navigates to the complete new hire list
 2. Verify that clicking 'View All' on exits navigates to the complete exit list
 3. Verify that the full list shows all records (not just the dashboard preview count)
 4. Verify that filter, sort, and search controls are available on the full list
 5. Verify that navigating back returns to the dashboard
 
 Negative:
 6. Verify that 'View All' when no records exist shows an empty full list
 
 UI Scenarios:
 7. Verify that 'View All' is displayed as a clickable link /button in the section header
 
 Edge Cases:
 8. Verify that the full list with 500+ records loads with pagination
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the full list loads within 3 seconds after clicking 'View All'

User Story: As a HR Admin, I should be able to click on employee to access their profile or onboarding/exit status.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to click on any employee name within the Workforce Movement section (new joiners or leaving employees) to directly access that employee's profile, onboarding status, or exit management details. This provides a quick navigation shortcut from the dashboard without needing to search for the employee separately. Clicking a new joiner navigates to their onboarding progress, while clicking a leaving employee navigates to their exit management page.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the HR Admin clicks a new joiner name, Then they navigate to the onboarding status page.
 2. If the HR Admin clicks a leaving employee name, Then they navigate to the exit management page.
 3. If the employee is in onboarding, Then the current step and progress are shown.
 4. If the employee is in exit process, Then the current exit step and remaining tasks are shown.
 5. After viewing the profile, Then the HR Admin can navigate back to the dashboard.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking a new joiner name navigates to their onboarding page
 2. Verify that clicking a leaving employee name navigates to their exit management page
 3. Verify that the onboarding page shows the current step (e.g., Step 2: Document Collection)
 4. Verify that the exit page shows remaining tasks (e.g., Asset Collection pending)
 5. Verify that the back navigation returns to the dashboard
 
 Negative:
 6. Verify that clicking an employee whose profile was deleted shows an error message
 
 UI Scenarios:
 7. Verify that employee names are displayed as clickable links (blue, underlined, or with cursor change)
 
 Edge Cases:
 8. Verify that clicking an employee while their profile is being edited by another admin is handled
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the employee profile or onboarding page loads within 2 seconds after clicking

User Story:
As a HR Admin, I should be able to view facilities with staff shortages showing
 - Facility name
 - Shift
 - Shortage count.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view a Staffing Management section on the Dashboard showing facilities that have staff shortages. Each entry displays the facility name, the shift that is understaffed (1st, 2nd, or 3rd), and the shortage count (how many staff members are needed). The Figma Shift Roaster Dashboard shows a similar concept with the 24/7 Coverage Monitor displaying 1st, 2nd, and 3rd shift coverage bars. This alert-driven section helps HR Admins proactively address understaffing before it impacts patient care or compliance.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Dashboard loads, Then the Staffing Management section shows facilities with shortages.
 2. If a facility has a staff shortage, Then it displays facility name, shift (1st/2nd/3rd), and shortage count.
  Else if no shortages exist, Then a 'All facilities fully staffed' message is shown.
 3. If multiple shifts are understaffed, Then each appears as a separate entry.
 4. After a shortage is resolved, Then the entry is removed and the list updates.
 5. If the HR Admin clicks on a shortage entry, Then they navigate to the shift calendar for that facility.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Staffing Management section shows facilities with staff shortages
 2. Verify that each entry shows facility name , shift, and shortage count 
 3. Verify that multiple understaffed shifts at the same facility show as separate entries
 4. Verify that resolving a shortage removes the entry from the list
 5. Verify that clicking a shortage entry navigates to the shift calendar
 
 Negative:
 6. Verify that no shortages shows 'All facilities fully staffed' message
 
 UI Scenarios:
 7. Verify that high shortage counts (5+) are highlighted in red for urgency
 8. Verify that shortage entries are sorted by count (highest first)
 
 Edge Cases:
 9. Verify that a facility with all three shifts understaffed shows three entries
 10. Verify that 20+ facilities with shortages display with scrolling
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the Staffing Management section loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As a HR Admin, I should be able to click alert to view shift details and request additional staff or approve overtime.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to click on a staffing shortage alert to view detailed shift information and take action by requesting additional staff or approving overtime for existing staff. When the admin clicks an alert, they see the specific shift details (time, current staff count, required count) and can either initiate a staff request or approve overtime hours for team members already scheduled. This is the action pathway from awareness (seeing the shortage) to resolution (filling the gap).
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the HR Admin clicks a shortage alert, Then detailed shift information is displayed.
 2. If 'Request Additional Staff' is clicked, Then a staff request form opens.
 3. If 'Approve Overtime' is clicked, Then an overtime approval form opens.
 4. After a staff request is submitted, Then the shortage status changes to 'Request Submitted' and managers are notified.
 5. After overtime is approved, Then the employee schedule updates and the shortage count may decrease.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking a shortage alert shows shift details
 2. Verify that 'Request Additional Staff' opens a staff request form
 3. Verify that 'Approve Overtime' opens an overtime approval form for eligible employees
 4. Verify that submitting a staff request changes the shortage status to 'Request Submitted'
 5. Verify that approving overtime updates the employee's schedule
 
 Negative:
 6. Verify that approving overtime for an employee already at max hours shows a warning
 7. Verify that requesting staff when no additional staff are available shows a notification
 
 UI Scenarios:
 8. Verify that the shift detail view shows a clear gap visualization (current vs required)
 
 Edge Cases:
 9. Verify that requesting staff and approving overtime simultaneously for the same shift is handled
 
 Security:
 10. Verify that only authorized HR Admins can approve overtime
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the shift detail view loads within 2 seconds after clicking the alert

User Story:
As a HR Admin, I should be able to view recent activities
 - New Hire
 - Shift Update
 - Exit
 - Compliance Review with person and time.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view a Recent Activities log on the Dashboard showing the latest workforce activities. Each activity entry shows the activity type (New Hire, Shift Update, Exit, Compliance Review), the person involved, and the timestamp. This chronological log provides a quick audit trail of what's happening across the HRMS module without needing to navigate to individual sections. The activity log serves as both a productivity tracker and an awareness tool for multi-admin environments.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the Dashboard loads, Then the Activity Log shows recent activities chronologically.
 2. If activities exist, Then each shows type, person, and timestamp.
  Else if no activities, Then 'No recent activities' message is shown.
 3. If the activity type is New Hire, Then a hire icon/label is shown.
  Else if Shift Update, Then a shift icon.
  Else if Exit, Then an exit icon.
  Else if Compliance Review, Then a compliance icon.
 4. If an activity is clicked, Then the admin navigates to the relevant detail.
 5. After a new activity occurs, Then it appears at the top of the log.
 6. If the log has more entries than the preview shows, Then a 'View All' option is available.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that the Activity Log shows recent activities on the Dashboard
 2. Verify that each entry shows activity type, person name, and timestamp
 3. Verify that activity types include New Hire, Shift Update, Exit, and Compliance Review
 4. Verify that activities are listed chronologically (newest first)
 5. Verify that clicking an activity navigates to the relevant detail page
 6. Verify that new activities appear at the top when the dashboard refreshes
 
 Negative:
 7. Verify that no activities shows 'No recent activities' message
 
 UI Scenarios:
 8. Verify that each activity type has a distinguishing icon or color
 9. Verify that timestamps are in readable format (e.g., '2 hours ago', 'Today at 3:30 PM')
 
 Edge Cases:
 10. Verify that 200+ activities display with scrolling or 'View All' option
 11. Verify that activities from multiple admins are all visible in the log
 
 Security:
 12. Verify that the activity log only shows activities from the admin's authorized facilities
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the Activity Log loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As a HR Admin, I should be able to click "View All" to see complete activity history with filters.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to click 'View All' on the Activity Log section to see the complete activity history beyond the dashboard preview. The full view includes all historical activities with filtering options (by date range, activity type, employee, facility) and search capability. This is important for auditing, compliance reporting, and tracking specific employee lifecycle events over time.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When 'View All' is clicked, Then the complete activity history loads.
 2. If filters are applied (date range, type, employee), Then only matching activities are shown.
 3. If a search term is entered, Then activities matching the term are displayed.
 4. If no activities match the filters, Then 'No activities found' message is shown.
 5. After viewing the full history, Then the HR Admin can navigate back to the dashboard.
 6. If the history has 1000+ entries, Then pagination is available.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking 'View All' navigates to the complete activity history
 2. Verify that all historical activities are displayed with type, person, timestamp
 3. Verify that date range filter works (e.g., filter to 'Last 7 Days')
 4. Verify that activity type filter works (e.g., filter to 'New Hire' only)
 5. Verify that searching by employee name shows matching activities
 6. Verify that navigating back returns to the dashboard
 
 Negative:
 7. Verify that filters with no matching results show 'No activities found'
 
 UI Scenarios:
 8. Verify that 'View All' is displayed as a clickable link
 9. Verify that filter controls are clearly visible on the full history page
 
 Edge Cases:
 10. Verify that 5000+ activity records load with pagination and acceptable performance
 
 Security:
 11. Verify that activity history only shows data from authorized facilities
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the full activity history loads within 3 seconds
 2. Verify that applying filters refreshes the list within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to Add an employee with its details.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to add a new employee to the system through the Employees page. The Figma screen shows a top navigation bar with the HRMS module highlighted, and the main content area displays the Employees listing page with an '+ Add Employee' button (blue, top-right). When clicked, the admin enters employee details including name, email, reporting manager, and role. The left sidebar shows navigation icons for Home, Employees, Shift Roaster, Leave, Label sections, Teams, and HR Admin. This story is critical because it is the first step in the hiring and onboarding workflow — without adding an employee record, no further onboarding steps can proceed.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the HR Admin clicks '+ Add Employee', Then the employee creation form opens.
 2. If the HR Admin fills all required fields and saves, Then the employee is created with 'In Progress' status.
  Else if required fields are missing, Then the system highlights the missing fields.
 3. After the employee is created, Then the employee appears in the Employee table
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking '+ Add Employee' opens the employee creation form
 2. Verify that entering valid details(Profile Picture , Employee ID, Employee Name, Salutation, Email, Role, Designation, Department, Phone Number, Country, Gender, Joining Date, Reporting To, Language, About, Address) and saving creates the employee
 3. Verify that the newly added employee appears in the list under the 'In Progress' tab
 4. Verify that the employee record shows correct name, email, and reporting manager
 
 Negative:
 5. Verify that submitting with employee name empty shows a validation error
 6. Verify that entering invalid email format shows an error
 7. Verify that entering a duplicate email already in the system shows a warning
 8. Verify that submitting without selecting a reporting manager shows a validation error
 
 UI Scenarios:
 9. Verify that '+ Add Employee' button at top-right of Employees page
10. Verify that After clicking on Add Employee button Add Employee window opens
 11. Verify that tabs 'All', 'Active', 'In Progress' are displayed above the table
 12. Verify that the table columns display: Employee ID, Name, Email, Reporting To,Role,Status, Actions
 
 Edge Cases:
 13. Verify that a name with 200+ characters is handled with max length validation
 14. Verify that special characters in name are accepted
 15. Verify that adding an employee concurrently with another admin is handled
 
 Security:
 16. Verify that a non-HR Admin user cannot access '+ Add Employee'
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the employee creation form loads within 1 seconds
 2. Verify that saving a new employee completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view Employees list with tabs for All, Active, and terminated employees.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view the complete employee list on the Employees page with tab-based filtering. The Figma screen shows a table with columns: # (ID like 856, 857), Name (with avatar), Email, Reporting To, and action icons. Three tabs above: 'All', 'Active', and 'In Progress'. Pagination at bottom ('Rows per page: 50, 1-10 of 14'). The Active tab shows active employees only, and the In Progress tab shows employees still in onboarding. This is essential for quick overview of workforce and onboarding status.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the HR Admin navigates to Employees page, Then the employee table displays with ID, Name, Email, Reporting To, Actions columns.
 2. If 'All' tab is selected, Then all employees are shown.
  Else if 'Active' tab is selected, Then only active employees are shown.
  Else if 'In Progress' tab is selected, Then only in-progress employees are shown.
 3. If employee count exceeds rows-per-page, Then pagination controls appear.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that the Employees page displays the table with all columns (ID, Name, Email, Reporting To,Role,Status, Actions)
 2. Verify that clicking 'All' tab shows all employees
 3. Verify that clicking 'Active' tab shows only active employees
 4. Verify that clicking 'Terminated' tab shows only Terminated employees
 5. Verify that pagination shows with page count

 Negative:
 7. Verify that viewing the list with no employees shows an empty state message
 8. Verify that clicking a tab with no matching employees shows an empty message
 
 UI Scenarios:
 9. Verify that employee names show profile avatars
 10. Verify that the three tabs are displayed above the table(All,Active,Terminated)
 11. Verify that the active tab is visually highlighted
 12. Verify that action icons are visible per row
 13. Verify that pagination section shows at bottom
 
 Edge Cases:
 14. Verify that very long email addresses are truncated with ellipsis
 15. Verify that 500+ employees loads with proper pagination
 16. Verify that switching tabs rapidly doesn't cause display issues
 
 Security:
 17. Verify that non-HR Admin cannot view terminated employee details
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the Employees page loads within 2 seconds with 500 records
 2. Verify that switching tabs refreshes within 1 second

User Story:
As an HR Admin, I should be able to view, add/select, send and delete onboarding forms for a new employee.
 ex; Background check report, Drug Screen report etc.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to manage onboarding forms during Step 2 of hiring. The Figma screen shows the employee profile with a stepper: 'Pre-Employment Screening' (completed checkmark), 'Mandatory Document Collection' (current), and 'Employment Activation' (pending). The Documentation tab is active showing forms like Background Check Report and Drug Screen Report that can be viewed, added, sent, and deleted. This step is critical for compliance — employees cannot proceed without completing required pre-employment documentation.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the HR Admin opens the Documentation tab during onboarding, Then the forms list with status is displayed.
 2. If the admin selects a form and clicks Send, Then it is delivered to the employee.
  Else if the admin clicks Delete, Then the form is removed after confirmation.
 3. If a new form is needed, Then the admin selects from available templates.
 4. After all mandatory forms are completed, Then the stepper advances.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that the Documentation tab shows the onboarding forms list
 2. Verify that the stepper shows 'Pre-Employment Screening', 'Mandatory Document Collection', 'Employment Activation'
 3. Verify that selecting a form (e.g., 'Background Check Report') from templates works
 4. Verify that clicking Send delivers the form to the employee
 5. Verify that clicking Delete shows confirmation and removes the form
 
 Negative:
 6. Verify that proceeding to Step 3 without completing mandatory forms is blocked
 7. Verify that deleting a submitted form shows a warning
 8. Verify that sending a form to employee with no email shows error
 
 UI Scenarios:
 9. Verify that the stepper shows checkmarks for completed stages
 10. Verify that the Documentation tab is highlighted
 11. Verify that employee profile header shows name, avatar, email, phone, address
 
 Edge Cases:
 12. Verify that adding the same form twice is prevented
 13. Verify that deleting all forms and proceeding shows mandatory forms message
 
 Security:
 14. Verify that only HR Admin role can manage onboarding forms
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the Documentation tab loads forms list within 2 seconds

User Story:
As an HR Admin, I should be able to upload, view, edit and delete mandatory employee documents during onboarding.
 ex; Licenses, Visa Details etc.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to manage mandatory documents during Step 3 (Mandatory Document Collection). The Figma shows the Documentation tab with document categories like Licenses and Visa Details. The admin uploads, views, edits metadata (expiry dates, categories), and deletes documents. Each entry shows type, upload date, and status. This ensures legally required documents are collected before employee activation.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the admin clicks Upload, Then a file selection dialog opens.
 2. If a valid file is selected, Then it uploads and appears in the list.
  Else if an invalid file type is selected, Then the system shows an error.
 3. If the admin clicks Edit, Then the metadata form opens.
 4. If the admin clicks Delete, Then confirmation dialog appears before removal.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking Upload opens a file selection dialog
 2. Verify that uploading documents adds it to the list
 3. Verify that the document shows name, type, upload date, status
 4. Verify that deleting shows confirmation and removes the document
 
 Negative:
 6. Verify that uploading a file >25MB shows a size error
 7. Verify that uploading .exe file is rejected
 8. Verify that uploading a zero-byte file shows an error
 9. Verify that proceeding to activation without all mandatory documents is blocked
 
 UI Scenarios:
 10. Verify that documents show type icons (PDF, image)
 11. Verify that upload progress is indicated
 12. Verify that Edit and Delete actions are visible per row
 
 Edge Cases:
 13. Verify that filenames with special characters are handled
 14. Verify that uploading same document twice shows duplicate warning
 
 Security:
 15. Verify that uploaded documents are scanned for malware
 16. Verify that document URLs require authentication
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that uploading a 10MB document completes within 5 seconds
 2. Verify that documents list loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to activate employee after verifying employee identity and background.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to activate an employee as the final onboarding step — Employment Activation. The Figma shows the stepper at 'Employment Activation' with Employee Summary displaying photo, name, role, title, and 'Official Start Date' field. A 'Convert to Active' button (blue, bottom-right) finalizes activation. 'Previous' button goes back. The header shows 'Send Welcome Email' section with text area. Once activated, status changes from 'In Progress' to 'Active' and portal access is granted.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the HR Admin reaches Employment Activation, Then Employee Summary is displayed.
 2. If all previous steps are completed, Then 'Convert to Active' button is enabled.
  Else if steps are incomplete, Then the button is disabled.
 3. If the admin clicks 'Convert to Active', Then status changes to 'Active'.
 4. If the admin sends welcome email with instructions, Then the email is delivered.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Employment Activation shows Employee Summary with correct name, role, Department, Supervisor, Official Start Date
 2. Verify that 'Official Start Date' accepts a valid date 
 3. Verify that 'Convert to Active' changes status to 'Active'
 4. Verify that activated employee appears in 'Active' tab
 5. Verify that entering first day instructions and clicking Send delivers the email
 6. Verify that 'Previous' navigates back to document collection step
 
 Negative:
 7. Verify that 'Convert to Active' without completing previous steps is blocked
 8. Verify that activating without setting start date shows validation error
 
 UI Scenarios:
 9. Verify that stepper shows checkmarks on completed stages
 10. Verify that 'Convert to Active' button is blue at bottom-right
 11. Verify that 'Previous' button is outlined next to it
 12. Verify that 'Send Welcome Email' section shows text area with placeholder
 
 Edge Cases:
 13. Verify that setting start date in the past is handled
 14. Verify that double-clicking 'Convert to Active' doesn't create duplicate activation
 
 Security:
 15. Verify that only HR Admin can activate employees
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that activation completes within 3 seconds
 2. Verify that welcome email sends within 5 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to send a welcome email to new employees.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to send a welcome email from the Employment Activation step. The Figma shows a 'Send Welcome Email' section at top-right of the employee profile with text area labeled 'Add first day instructions, and onboarding details' and a 'Send' button. The email provides portal access credentials and first-day information. This is a critical communication step connecting the employee to the system.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the HR Admin views Employment Activation, Then 'Send Welcome Email' section is visible.
 2. If the admin enters instructions and clicks Send, Then the email is delivered.
 3. After email is sent, Then a success confirmation is shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that 'Send Welcome Email' section is visible on Employment Activation
 2. Verify that entering instructions and clicking Send delivers the email
 3. Verify that a success message is shown after sending
 
 Negative:
 5. Verify that sending to employee with invalid email shows error

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to resend welcome email.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to resend the welcome email if the original was not received or needs updating. The resend action is available from the employee profile. This handles cases where email delivery fails or employee needs updated first-day information.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If a welcome email was previously sent, Then the Resend option is available.
 2. When the admin clicks Resend, Then the email is re-delivered.
 3. After succesful resend, Then the system shows confirmation.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Resend option is available for employees who were previously sent a welcome email
 2. Verify that clicking Resend re-delivers the welcome email
 3. Verify that success confirmation is shown after resend
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that Resend is not available for employees never sent a welcome email
 5. Verify that resending to employee with no email shows error
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that Resend button is clearly visible and distinguishable from initial Send
 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that resending 10 times is tracked in email history
 8. Verify that resending after updating employee email sends to the new address
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that resending completes within 5 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to grant role-based access to employee.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to assign role-based portal access to an employee after activation. The admin selects from pre-defined roles (LPN, RN, Admin, Supervisor) that determine module and feature access. This is critical for security — employees see only what their role permits.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the HR Admin opens role assignment, Then current and available roles are displayed.
 2. If a new role is selected and saved, Then permissions update.
 3. After role assignment, Then the employee's portal reflects the new role.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:

 2. Verify that selecting role 'LPN' and saving grants corresponding permissions
 3. Verify that the employee portal shows only modules permitted by the assigned role
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that saving without selecting a role shows a validation error
 5. Verify that a non-HR Admin cannot change employee roles
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that role selection shows all available roles in dropdown
 7. Verify that currently assigned role is pre-selected
 
 Edge Cases:
 8. Verify that changing from 'Admin' to 'LPN' immediately restricts access
 9. Verify that assigning a role to an inactive employee is handled
 
 Security:
 10. Verify that role changes are logged in audit trail
 11. Verify that admin cannot assign themselves a higher role
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that role assignment saves within 2 seconds

User Story:
As a HR Admin, I should be able to view & edit employee information.

 - Other Details

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view all the employee documents in documents tab.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view all documents in the Documents tab of the employee profile. Documents are organized showing type, Expiry Date, status (active/expiring/expired), and actions. This provides centralized compliance documentation view.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the HR Admin clicks Documents tab, Then documents are displayed in Mandatory and other tab.
2. If HR Admin clicks on other tab then it shows other documents
    Else Mandatory tab is selected by default.
 2. If a document is active, Then green indicator shown.
  Else if expiring soon, Then amber indicator.
  Else if expired, Then red indicator.
3. When the HR Admin clicks the View (eye) icon in the Action buttons on any document row, Then the document opens in a preview.

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to manage employee documents
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to Add, edit, download, and delete employee documents. This ensures records stay current and compliant with regulatory requirements.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):

2. When File dialog opens then Add document name , category and file

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view employee shifts list in the employee profile
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view all shifts assigned to a specific employee from their profile. The shifts list shows dates, times, types (1st, 2nd, 3rd), and status (assigned, completed, Day Off, Leave). This helps understand schedule history.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the HR Admin opens the Shifts section, Then all assigned shifts are displayed.

  Else if Leave, Then 'Leave' with type shown.
  Else Then shift type (1st/2nd/3rd) with time range.
3.If a shift was completed within scheduled hours, Then status shows "Completed" in green and Overtime shows
  Else if the employee worked beyond scheduled hours, Then status shows "Overtime" in orange and the Overtime column displays the extra hours

 4. Verify that Leave entries show 'Leave' with approval status

 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that shift types are color-coded matching Shift Calendar
 7. Verify that the status is also color-coded 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that 365+ shift entries (full year) loads properly
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that shifts list loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to manage employee shifts.

Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When Add Shift is clicked, Then the assignment form opens.
 2. If valid details are saved, Then the shift appears in the list.

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view Leave History list of the employee in the employee profile
Description of User Story: This story enables viewing an employee's complete leave history from their profile showing past and current requests with dates, type, duration, and approval status.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When Leave History is opened, Then all leave records are displayed in chronological order.
2.Each record should show Request Type, Start Date, End Date, Description, and Status.
3.If the leave is approved, Then ‘Approved’ status is shown.
Else if pending, Then ‘Pending’ is shown.
Else if rejected, Then ‘Rejected’ is shown.
4.If there are multiple leave records, Then pagination should be displayed.
5.When no leave records are available, Then an empty state message should be shown.
6.If the user navigates between pages, Then the correct set of records should be displayed.
7.If a leave record has a long description, Then it should be properly displayed without UI break.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:

 2. Verify that each entry shows type, from-to dates, duration, status
 3. Verify that entries are sorted newest first
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that no leave records shows empty state
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that status uses colors (green=Approved, orange=Pending, red=Rejected)
 
 Edge Cases:
 6. Verify that 100+ leave records loads properly
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that leave history loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to manage employee leave history actions.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to edit leave records, cancel approved leaves, and adjust balances. Ensures leave records stay accurate.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the HR Admin edits a leave record, Then the updated details should be saved successfully.
2.If the HR Admin cancels an approved leave, Then the leave days should be restored to the balance.
3.After any action (edit/cancel), Then the leave balance should be recalculated correctly.
4.If changes are made, Then they should be reflected in the Leave History.
5.If no changes are made and save is clicked, Then no error should occur and data should remain unchanged.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that editing a leave record updates dates and type
 2. Verify that canceling approved leave restores days to balance
 3. Verify that balance recalculates after modification
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that editing a past leave shows warning
 5. Verify that canceling ongoing leave shows appropriate message
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that edit and cancel actions are accessible from leave history
 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that modifying leave in processed payroll triggers recalculation notice
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that balance recalculation completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view All Timecards of the employee in employee profile.
Description of User Story: This story enables viewing all employee timecards showing hours worked, clock times, and approval status. Essential for payroll processing.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the Timecards section is opened, Then all timecards should be displayed.
2.Each record should show Week, Regular Hours, Overtime, Total Hours, and Status.
3.If the timecard is approved, Then ‘Approved’ status should be shown.
Else if pending, Then ‘Pending’ status should be shown.
Else if rejected, Then ‘Rejected’ status should be shown.
4.If overtime exists, Then it should be displayed correctly in the Overtime column.
5.If multiple timecards exist, Then pagination should be displayed.
6.When navigating between pages, Then correct timecard records should be shown.
7.If no timecards are available, Then an empty state message should be displayed.
Test Scenarios:

 2. Verify that each shows pay period, total hours, approval status

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to perform the actions on timecards of the employee.
Description of User Story: This story enables approving, rejecting, editing, and exporting timecards. Approved timecards flow to payroll.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the HR Admin clicks Approve, Then the timecard should move to Approved status.
2.When the HR Admin clicks Reject, Then a reason field should appear and the timecard should be sent back to the employee.
3.When the HR Admin clicks Edit, Then the hours should be editable with a mandatory reason.
4.After any action (Approve/Reject/Edit), Then the updated status should be reflected in the Timecards list.
5.If no changes are made and action is attempted, Then no error should occur and data should remain unchanged.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that approving changes status to 'Approved'
 2. Verify that rejecting with reason sends it back to employee
 3. Verify that editing hours and saving updates with audit note
 
 Negative:

 5. Verify that approving already-processed timecard shows warning
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that Approve, Reject, Edit actions visible on pending timecards
 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that editing hours adjusts overtime calculations
 
 Security:
 8. Verify that approvals are logged with approver name and timestamp
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that timecard approval completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view all shift entries including Day Off & Leave entries in day, week and month view.

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view shift timeline.
Description of User Story: This story enables viewing a shift timeline showing the daily schedule as horizontal bars across a time axis. Provides quick coverage overview and helps identify gaps.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When shift timeline is opened, Then shifts display as bars across time axis.
2. When the "Legends" button is clicked, Then a legend panel appears showing color codes and labels for all shift types (1st, 2nd, 3rd), Leave statuses (Applied, Approved), and Day Off so the user can identify each category.

 6. Verify that 20+ concurrent shifts display without overlap issues
