User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to filter calendar by date range, employees, shifts, department and facility
Description of User Story: This story enables filtering the Shift Calendar. The Figma Filter screen shows a panel with 'Filters' header, count badge (0), 'Clear All' link. Left sidebar: 'Employees' category with count, placeholder 'Label' categories. Right panel: search field 'Search Label', 'Select All' checkbox, individual employee checkboxes (Kristin Watson, Darlene Robertson with avatars). Close and Apply (blue) buttons at bottom.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the filter icon is clicked, Then the filter panel should open with all categories (Date Range, Employees, Shifts, Department).
2.If a date range is selected and Apply is clicked, Then the calendar should be filtered based on the selected dates.
3.If employees are selected and Apply is clicked, Then the calendar should show data only for those employees.
4.If ‘Select All’ is checked, Then all employees should be selected.
5.If ‘Clear All’ is clicked, Then all applied filters should be removed and default view should be shown.
6.If Apply is clicked without selecting any filters, Then no changes should be applied.
7.When the filter panel is closed and reopened, Then previously applied filters should be retained (if not cleared).
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking filter icon opens the filter panel
 2. Verify that selecting employee and clicking Apply shows only her shifts
 3. Verify that selecting multiple employees shows all their shifts
 4. Verify that 'Select All' selects all employees
 5. Verify that 'Clear All' removes all active filters
 6. Verify that search field filters employee list in real-time
 
 Negative:
 7. Verify that filters matching no shifts shows empty calendar
 8. Verify that closing panel without Apply doesn't change view
 
 UI Scenarios:
 9. Verify that filter panel shows 'Filters' header with count badge
 10. Verify that categories listed in left sidebar with counts
 11. Verify that employee checkboxes show avatars
 12. Verify that Close and Apply buttons at bottom
 
 Edge Cases:
 13. Verify that 100+ employees in filter list performs search without lag
 14. Verify that filters persist when switching calendar views
 
 Security:
 15. Verify that filter only shows employees the admin has permission to view
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that applying filters updates calendar within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to approve / reject employee leave request.

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view leave request details for a specific request
Description of User Story: This story enables viewing detailed leave request information. The expanded view shows employee name/avatar, leave type badge, date range, duration, reason, leave quota progress bar, and dual approval tracking (Supervisor/HR). 'Request Info' button provides additional context.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the HR Admin clicks "View Details" on a leave request,
Then the modal displays the employee's profile photo, full name, designation (e.g., "LPN"), email address, and phone number.
2.When the modal opens,
Then it shows the leave request date, total number of days, "From" date, and "To" date.
3.If the leave request is Approved,
Then a green "Approved" banner is displayed with the HR Approval badge showing "Approved" in green.
Else if the leave request is Rejected,
Then a red/pink "Rejected" banner is displayed.
Else if the leave request is Pending,
Then the status shows as "Waiting" in the HR Approval section.
4.If the leave request status is Rejected,
Then a "Reason" section is displayed below the status banner showing the rejection reason text.
Else if the status is Approved or Pending,
Then the rejection reason section is NOT displayed.
5.When the modal shows a leave request,
Then a "Reason" button with an info icon (i) is displayed next to the leave request date, allowing the user to view the leave reason.
6.If a shift change is associated with the leave request,
Then the modal displays a "Shift Changed" section showing the employee's name, designation, email, phone, shift change date, "From" shift (name + time), "To" shift (name + time), "Starts From" date, and "Ends On" date.
Else Then the shift change section is NOT displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1.Verify that clicking "View Details" opens the modal with employee name, designation, email, phone, and profile photo. 
2.Verify that the leave request shows correct date, day count, "From" date, and "To" date.
3.Verify that an Approved request shows green "Approved" banner and HR Approval badge; a Rejected request shows red/pink "Rejected" banner with rejection reason displayed below. 
4.Verify that when a shift change is linked, the "Shift Changed" section displays From shift, To shift, start date, and end date — and is hidden when no shift change exists.
 Negative:
 4. Verify that viewing Rejected/Approved request shows appropriate message
 5.Verify that if the employee has no profile photo, a default placeholder avatar is displayed.
 UI Scenarios:
6.Verify that the "Approved" banner has green background and "Rejected" banner has red/pink background. 6. Verify that Reason text displays below employee info
7.Verify that the profile photo is displayed as a circular avatar with the designation shown as a gray rounded pill.
8.Verify that the day count is displayed inside a bordered card with "From" and "To" labels properly aligned. 
 Edge Cases:
 9. Verify that a leave request of 1 day displays "1 Day" (singular) and same From/To date.
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that expanding details loads within 1 second

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view a list of all employee performance reviews.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view a list of all performance reviews across employees. The list shows employee name, review period, reviewer, rating, and status (pending, completed, approved). This provides an organizational overview of performance evaluation progress.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When Performance Reviews is opened, Then all reviews display in a list.
 2. When the HR Admin navigates to the Performance Reviews section,
Then all reviews are displayed in a table showing employee photo, name, ID, role, department, joined date, days left, status, and action buttons (View / Approve).
3. When the HR Admin clicks the "All" tab, Then all performance reviews are displayed regardless of status.
Else if the HR Admin clicks "Approved", Then only reviews with "Approved" status are shown.
Else if the HR Admin clicks "Pending", Then only reviews with "Pending" status are shown.
Else if the HR Admin clicks "Rejected", Then only reviews with "Rejected" status are shown.
4.If the review status is Approved, Then a green "Approved" badge is displayed.
Else if the status is Pending, Then an orange "Pending" badge is displayed.
Else if the status is Rejected, Then a red "Rejected" badge is displayed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Performance Reviews list shows all reviews
 2.Verify that navigating to Performance Reviews displays all reviews in a table with columns: Name (photo + ID), Role, Department, Joined Date, Days Left, Status, and Action (View / Approve).
 3. Verify that clicking "All" tab shows all reviews, clicking "Approved" shows only approved, clicking "Pending" shows only pending, and clicking "Rejected" shows only rejected reviews.
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that no reviews shows empty state
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that rating displays as stars or numerical score
6.Verify that Approved status displays a green badge, Pending displays an orange badge, and Rejected displays a red badge 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that 500+ reviews loads with pagination
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that reviews list loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to approve employee performance
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to approve employee performance reviews submitted by supervisors. Approval finalizes the review and makes it visible to the employee. This is part of the performance management workflow.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the admin clicks Approve on a submitted review, Then the review is finalized.
 2. After approval, Then the employee can see their review in their portal.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking Approve on a submitted review finalizes it
 2. Verify that the approved review becomes visible to the employee
 3. Verify that the review status changes to 'Approved'
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that approving an already-approved review shows 'Already approved' message
 5. Verify that approving a draft review that hasn't been submitted is blocked
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that Approve button is 'Green' and visible only on submitted reviews
 
 Security:
 7. Verify that approval actions are logged with approver name and timestamp
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that approval completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view employee performance details
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view detailed performance review information for a specific employee including ratings, comments, goals, achievements, and improvement areas. This gives a complete picture of the employee's performance evaluation.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the HR Admin clicks "View" on a performance review,
Then the modal displays the employee's profile photo, Resident Name, Role, Department, Joined Date, Contact number, and Email
2.When the modal is opened,
Then the review sections (Rating, Strengths, Areas of Improvement, Goals, Comments) are displayed as read-only text — the HR Admin cannot edit the content, only view and approve or cancel.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking a review opens the full details
 2. Verify that details show ratings, reviewer comments, goals, achievements
 3. Verify that improvement areas are listed

 5. Verify that ratings display as visual indicators (stars, bars)
 6. Verify that profile photo is properly  aligned 
 
 Edge Cases:
 6. Verify that very long review comments (2000+ chars) display properly
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that review details load within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to schedule orientation for new employee.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to schedule an orientation session for a new employee. The admin selects date, time, location, facilitator, and orientation type. This ensures new hires receive proper introduction to the facility, policies, and procedures before starting their role.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the admin clicks Schedule Orientation, Then the scheduling form opens.
 2. If all details are entered and saved, Then the orientation is scheduled and employee notified.
 3. After scheduling, Then the orientation appears in the employee's onboarding timeline.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Schedule Orientation form shows date, time, location, facilitator fields
 2. Verify that entering valid details and saving creates the orientation
 3. Verify that the employee receives a notification
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that scheduling for past date shows validation error
 5. Verify that scheduling without selecting facilitator shows error
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that date picker and time slot fields are present
 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that scheduling when facilitator has a conflict shows warning
 8. Verify that scheduling orientation for multiple new hires at same slot is allowed
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that orientation scheduling saves within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to assign training modules to employee.
Description of User Story: This story enables assigning training modules (e.g., HIPAA training, safety protocols, system training) to employees. The admin selects from available modules and assigns them with a completion deadline. This ensures employees complete required training for compliance.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When Assign Training is clicked, Then the module selection form opens.
 2. If modules are selected with deadlines and saved, Then they appear in the employee's training list.
 3. After assignment, Then the employee is notified of pending training.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking Assign Training shows available modules
 2. Verify that selecting 'HIPAA Training' with deadline '2026-12-31' and saving assigns it
 3. Verify that the assigned module appears in the employee's training list
 
 Negative:

 5. Verify that setting a past deadline shows validation error
 6. Verify that assigning a duplicate module shows 'Already assigned' warning

 7. Verify that module list shows module name, description, and estimated duration

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to update training completion status for employee.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to update the completion status of assigned training modules. The admin can mark modules as completed, in-progress, or not started, and upload completion certificates if applicable.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the admin updates status to 'Completed', Then completion date is recorded.
  Else if 'In Progress', Then progress percentage can be set.
 2. If a certificate is uploaded, Then it is attached to the record.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that updating status from 'Not Started' to 'In Progress' saves correctly
 2. Verify that updating to 'Completed' records the completion date
 3. Verify that uploading a completion certificate attaches it to the record

 6. Verify that status dropdown shows: Not Started, In Progress, Completed

 7. Verify that changing status back from 'Completed' to 'In Progress' clears the completion date
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that status update saves within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to create and submit an employee separation request for an employee
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to initiate an employee separation process by creating and submitting a separation request. The admin specifies the separation type (resignation, termination, retirement), effective date, reason, and any notes. This starts the formal exit process.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When Create Separation Request is clicked, Then the form opens with separation type, date, reason fields.
 2. If all fields are filled and submitted, Then the exit process initiates.
 3. After submission, Then the employee's status changes to 'Separation In Progress'.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking Create Separation Request opens the form
 2. Verify that selecting type 'Resignation', date '2026-12-31', and reason saves correctly
 3. Verify that submission changes employee status to 'Separation In Progress'
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that submitting without separation type shows error
 5. Verify that submitting without effective date shows error
 6. Verify that creating a separation for already-separated employee is blocked
 
 UI Scenarios:
 7. Verify that separation type shows: Resignation, Termination, Retirement
 8. Verify that date picker allows future dates only
 
 Edge Cases:
 9. Verify that creating separation for an employee currently on leave is handled with notice
 
 Security:
 10. Verify that only HR Admin can create separation requests
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that separation request submission completes within 3 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view employees exit management list
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view a list of all employees in the exit process showing their name, separation type, effective date, exit stage, and completion status.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the HR Admin navigates to Exit Management, Then all exit requests are displayed as cards showing employee photo, name, ID, status badge, role, last working day, and exit reason.
2. If the exit status is Pending, Then an orange "Pending" badge is shown. Else if Approved, Then a green badge. Else if Rejected, Then a red badge.
3. When the HR Admin clicks on an exit request card, Then the detailed exit information is displayed with options to approve or reject the request.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1.Verify that navigating to Exit Management displays all exit requests with employee photo, name, ID, status, role, last day, and reason
2.Verify that Pending status shows orange badge, Approved shows green badge, and Rejected shows red badge. 

 Negative:
3.Verify that clicking on an exit request card opens the detail view with approve/reject options. 

 UI Scenarios:
 4. Verify that exit stages are visually indicated (e.g., stepper or status badges)
 
 Edge Cases:
 5. Verify that 50+ exits display with pagination
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that exit management list loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to select employee from list to view full exit details.
Description of User Story: This story enables viewing complete exit details for a specific employee including separation type, dates, reason, exit interview status, final pay, asset collection status, and portal access status.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the HR Admin clicks on an employee from the exit list, Then the right panel displays the employee's name, status, resignation date, last date, and step completion progress (e.g., "2/5 Steps Completed").
2.When the detail panel loads, Then all exit steps are displayed in order — Step 1: Exit Interview, Step 2: Final Pay Calculation, Step 3: Benefits Termination, Step 4: Company Assets — each showing its checklist items and completion status.
3.When the HR Admin clicks "Mark As Complete" on an active step, Then that step is marked complete and the progress counter updates.
If the HR Admin clicks "Skip", Then the step is skipped and the next step becomes active.
4.When the HR Admin clicks "Save Progress", Then the current state is saved without finalizing.
If the HR Admin clicks "Finalize Exit", Then all steps are reviewed and the exit process is closed with status updated to complete.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking an employee from the left list loads the right panel with correct name, status, resignation date, last date, and step progress.
2. Verify that completed steps show green checkmark, in-progress step shows blue circle, and pending steps show gray circle. 
3. Verify that clicking "Mark As Complete" on a step marks it complete, updates the progress counter, and activates the next step.
4. Verify that clicking "Skip" bypasses the current step and moves to the next step.
5.Verify that "Save Progress" saves without closing and "Finalize Exit" completes the exit process and updates the employee status.
 Negative:
 6. Verify that viewing details of completed exit shows read-only information
 
 UI Scenarios:
7.Verify that the selected employee card in the left list is highlighted with a blue/orange border. 
8.Verify that each step displays its step number label with title and checklist items.
9.Verify that "Skip" button is styled as an outlined button and "Mark As Complete" is styled as a filled primary button.
10.Verify that the progress indicator  with a progress bar is displayed at the top-right of the detail panel.
11.Verify that "Save Progress" is styled as an outlined button and "Finalize Exit" is styled as a red/primary action button at the bottom-right.
 Edge Cases:
 12. Verify that an exit with all steps completed shows 'Complete' status for each
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that exit details load within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to conduct exit interview
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to conduct and document an exit interview with a separating employee. The admin records feedback, reasons for leaving, suggestions, and overall experience. This data helps improve retention and workplace culture.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When exit interview section is opened, Then the interview form displays.
 2. If responses are entered and saved, Then the interview is marked as completed.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that the exit interview form shows fields for feedback, reasons, suggestions
 2. Verify that entering responses and saving records the interview
 3. Verify that the interview status changes to 'Completed'
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that saving without entering mandatory feedback shows error
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that the interview form has clear sections for different feedback categories
 
 Edge Cases:
 6. Verify that very long feedback responses (3000+ chars) are saved properly
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that interview saves within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to view the final pay calculation and mark the employee exit process as complete to finalize the separation.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to view the final pay calculation including remaining salary, unused PTO payout, deductions, and any outstanding balances. Once verified, the admin marks the exit process as complete which finalizes the separation.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When final pay section is viewed, Then calculations show salary, PTO payout, deductions, net pay.
 2. If the admin clicks 'Mark as Complete', Then the exit process is finalized.
 3. After finalization, Then the employee record moves to 'Terminated' status.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that final pay shows remaining salary, PTO payout, deductions, net amount
 2. Verify that calculations are mathematically correct
 3. Verify that clicking 'Mark as Complete' finalizes the step of final pay calculation.
 4. Verify that you moves to next step after clicking 'Mark as Complete'
 
 Negative:
 5. Verify that Clicking on Finalize Exit without completing all exit steps is blocked
 6. Verify that marking complete with pending asset collection shows warning
 
 UI Scenarios:
 7. Verify that final pay breakdown is displayed with checklist
 
 Edge Cases:
 8. Verify that negative net pay (deductions > salary) is handled appropriately
 9. Verify that the PTO payout is calculated correctly based on the unused leave days an employee has earned.
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that final pay calculation loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to revoke employee portal access on separation.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to revoke an employee's system access upon separation. This immediately disables their login and removes their ability to access any portal features. Critical for security to prevent unauthorized access after an employee leaves.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.If the admin clicks 'Revoke Access', then the employee's login is disabled immediately.
2.After revocation, then any active sessions are terminated.
3.If the employee tries to log in after access is revoked, then access is denied with an appropriate message.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking 'Revoke Access' disables the employee's login
 2. Verify that the employee can no longer log in after revocation
 3. Verify that any active sessions are terminated immediately
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that revoking access for an employee who already has no access shows message
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that 'Revoke Access' shows a confirmation dialog before execution
 
 Edge Cases:
 6. Verify that revoking access while the employee is currently logged in terminates their session
 
 Security:
 7. Verify that revocation is logged with timestamp and admin name
 8. Verify that revoked credentials cannot be used via API
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that access revocation takes effect within 5 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to terminate employee benefits
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to terminate employee benefits (health insurance, dental, vision, retirement contributions) as part of the separation process. The admin selects which benefits to terminate and sets the termination effective date.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the admin selects benefits to terminate, then the termination effective date is set.
2.If confirmed, then benefits are scheduled for termination.
3.After the effective date, then the benefits are deactivated.
4.If the effective date is in the future, then benefits remain active until that date.
5.If the admin tries to set a past date, then a validation message is shown.
6.After deactivation, then the employee should no longer be able to use or view the terminated benefits
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that selecting benefits for termination works
 2. Verify that setting termination effective date saves correctly
 3. Verify that benefits are deactivated after the effective date
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that terminating benefits without selecting any shows error
 5. Verify that setting termination date before separation date shows warning
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that benefits are listed with checkboxes for selection
 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that notification is triggered upon benefit termination
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that benefit termination saves within 1 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to manage employee asset collection.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to manage the collection of company assets from a separating employee. Assets include laptops, keys, badges, uniforms, and other company property. The admin tracks which assets have been returned and which are outstanding.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When asset collection section is opened, Then all assigned assets display.
 2. If an asset is marked as returned, Then its status updates to 'Returned'.
 3. If all assets are returned, Then asset collection is marked complete.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that the asset list shows all company assets assigned to the employee
 2. Verify that marking an asset as returned updates its status
 3. Verify that all assets returned marks collection as complete
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that completing exit without all assets returned shows warning
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that each asset shows name, type, assigned date, and return status
 
 Edge Cases:
 6. Verify that an asset reported as damaged is tracked with a damage note
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that asset status update saves within 2 seconds

User Story: As an HR Admin, I should be able to mark asset collection as complete.
Description of User Story: This story enables the HR Admin to mark the overall asset collection process as complete once all assets have been returned or accounted for. This is a checkpoint in the exit workflow before final pay can be processed.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If all assets are returned, then the 'Mark As Complete' button is enabled.
Else if assets are outstanding, then the button is disabled or shows a warning.
 2. If the admin clicks 'Skip', then the system allows moving to the next step without completing asset return.
 3. After marking complete, Then the exit workflow advances to final pay.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that 'Mark Collection Complete' is enabled when all assets are returned
 2. Verify that clicking it finalizes the collection step
 3. Verify that the exit workflow advances after completion
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that marking complete with outstanding assets shows warning with list of missing assets
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that the button state changes based on asset return status
 
 Edge Cases:
 6. Verify that marking complete when one asset is damaged but accounted for is allowed with note
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that completion saves within 2 seconds

User Story:
As an Employee, I should be able to view cards
- upcoming shift card
- Leave Balance card
- Pending Requests card
- Performance Card
Description of User Story: This story allows an employee to view important information through dashboard cards in one place. The employee can see details of their upcoming shifts, current leave balance, pending requests, and performance status. These cards help the employee quickly understand their schedule, leave usage, request status, and overall performance without navigating to different sections.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the employee dashboard loads, Then all cards are displayed.
2.When data is available, Then Upcoming Shift card shows next shift date, time, and details.
Else if no shift is scheduled, Then an empty state message is shown.
3.When data is available, Then Leave Balance card shows total, used, and remaining leaves.
Else if no leave data, Then default or zero values are shown.
4.When there are requests, Then Pending Requests card shows all pending requests with status.
Else if no pending requests, Then an empty state message is shown.
5.When performance data is available, Then Performance card shows employee performance details.
Else if no data, Then an empty or default state is shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1.Verify that the Home page loads with all dashboard cards
2.Verify that Upcoming Shift card shows next shift details when available
3.Verify that Leave Balance card shows total, used, and remaining leaves
4.Verify that Pending Requests card shows list of pending requests
5.Verify that Performance card shows employee performance details
 
Negative:
6.Verify that Upcoming Shift card shows 'No upcoming shifts' when no shift is scheduled
7.Verify that Pending Requests card shows 'No pending requests' when no requests exist
8.Verify that Performance card shows empty or default state when no data is available
 
UI Scenarios:
9.Verify that all cards are displayed properly on the dashboard
10.Verify that all cards are aligned in a proper grid layout
11.Verify that each card has a title, content section, and proper formatting
12.Verify that card content is readable and not overlapping
 
Edge Cases:
13.Verify that Leave Balance card shows zero values for a new employee with no leave data
14.Verify that dashboard loads properly when all cards have data
15.Verify that dashboard updates when new request or shift is added
 
 Security:
 16. Verify that the dashboard only shows data for the logged-in employee
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that the Employee Dashboard loads within 3 seconds
 2. Verify that all six cards render within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to view weekly schedule with week/day navigation and shift details for each day.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to view their shift schedule in the My Shifts section of the Employee Portal. The schedule supports day, week, and month views with navigation arrows to move between periods. Each day shows the assigned shift type (1st/2nd/3rd), time range, and Day Off/Leave status. This helps employees plan their personal schedules around work commitments.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the employee opens My Shifts, Then the weekly schedule loads with shift details.
 2. If Day view is selected, Then only the current day's shift is shown.
  Else if Week, Then the current week.
 3. If a day is a Day Off, Then 'Day Off' label is shown.
  Else if Leave, Then 'Leave' with type is shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that My Shifts shows the weekly schedule with shift details
 2. Verify that each day shows shift type
 3. Verify that navigation arrows move to previous/next week
 4. Verify that switching to Month view shows the full month calendar
 5. Verify that switching to Day view shows only today's shift
 6. Verify that Day Off entries show 'Day Off' label
 7. Verify that Leave entries show leave type and approval status
 
 Negative:
 8. Verify that a week with no shifts shows 'No shifts scheduled' for each day
 
 UI Scenarios:
 9. Verify that the My Shifts icon is highlighted in sidebar when active
 10. Verify that Day/Week toggle is visible at the top
 11. Verify that shift types are color-coded matching the Shift Calendar
 
 Edge Cases:
 12. Verify that viewing a month far in the future loads without errors
 13. Verify that a day with multiple shifts (e.g., split shift) shows all entries
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the weekly schedule loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to clock in/out with timer display and break marking.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to clock in at the start of their shift, clock out at the end, and mark breaks during the shift. The Clock In/Out card on the dashboard shows a timer display with the current elapsed time, a prominent Clock In button (before clocking in) or Clock Out button (after clocking in), and a Break button to mark break start/end. The timer tracks total work hours minus break time for accurate timecard records.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the employee clicks 'Clock In', Then the timer starts displaying elapsed time.
 2. If the employee clicks 'Break' during shift, Then break timer starts and work timer pauses.
 3. If 'End Break' is clicked, Then work timer resumes and break timer pauses.
 4. If 'Clock Out' is clicked, Then the total hours (work minus breaks) are recorded .
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking 'Clock In' starts the timer and shows elapsed time
 2. Verify that the Clock In button changes to Clock Out after clocking in
 3. Verify that clicking 'Break' pauses the work timer and starts the break timer
 4. Verify that clicking 'End Break' resumes the work timer
 5. Verify that clicking 'Clock Out' stops the timer and records total hours
 6. Verify that the timecard entry shows total work hours minus break time
 
 Negative:
 7. Verify that clicking 'Clock In' before scheduled shift time shows a warning or is allowed with note
 8. Verify that clicking 'Clock Out' without clocking in is not possible
 9. Verify that clocking in twice without clocking out is prevented
 
 UI Scenarios:
 10. Verify that the timer display is prominently shown on the dashboard card
 11. Verify that Clock In button is green and Clock Out is red for visual distinction
 12. Verify that break indicator is visible while on break
 
 Edge Cases:
 13. Verify that the timer continues if the browser tab is minimized
 14. Verify that if the employee forgets to clock out, the timecard shows a 'missed punch' flag
 15. Verify that taking multiple breaks during a shift accumulates total break time correctly
 
 Security:
 16. Verify that clock in/out timestamps cannot be manipulated via browser developer tools
Performance Scenarios:
1. Verify that clock in/out action completes within 1 second
 2. Verify that the timer display updates every second without lag

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to view today's tasks with priority, status, assigned by details and mark tasks complete.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to view their assigned tasks for today on the Tasks section. Each task shows the task title, priority level (High/Medium/Low), current status (Pending/In Progress/Completed), who assigned it, and a deadline. The employee can mark tasks as complete by clicking a checkbox or complete button. This helps employees manage their daily workload and track progress.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the employee opens the dashboard, Then today's tasks are shown in the Tasks card with priority, status, assigned by, and deadline.
2.If tasks are available, Then task details are displayed correctly in the card.
Else if no tasks are available, Then 'No tasks available' message is shown.
3.When the employee marks a task as complete from the dashboard, Then its status changes to 'Completed'.
4.After marking a task as complete, Then the pending task count is updated on the card.
5.After completing all tasks, Then 'No pending tasks' message is shown on the dashboard card.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
1.Verify that Tasks card on dashboard shows today's tasks with title, priority, status, assigned by, and deadline
2.Verify that clicking 'Mark Complete' on a task updates its status to 'Completed'
3.Verify that completing a task updates the Pending Tasks count on the dashboard
4.Verify that tasks are sorted by priority (High first)
 
Negative:
5.Verify that a task with no due date still displays in the Tasks card without error
6.Verify that marking a task complete without required notes (if mandatory) shows an error
 
UI Scenarios:
7.Verify that priority levels are color-coded (High = red, Medium = yellow, Low = green)
8.Verify that Tasks card is properly visible and structured on the dashboard
9.Verify that completed tasks are visually distinguished (strikethrough or gray)
 
Edge Cases:
10.Verify that marking a task complete and then undoing it restores the task in the card
11.Verify that a large number of tasks (50+) are handled properly within the card (scroll/limit)
 
 Security:
12.Verify that employees can only see their own assigned tasks in the dashboard card
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that Tasks page loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to view notice board with notices and category tags on the employee dashboard
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to view facility notices on the Notice Board section of the dashboard. Each notice has a title, content, category tag (e.g., Announcement, Policy Update, Event, Safety), and posted date. This keeps employees informed about facility-wide communications.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the employee views the Notice Board on the dashboard, Then notices are displayed with title, category tag, and date.
2.When the employee clicks on a notice, Then the full content expands or opens.
3.If notices are available, Then all details are displayed correctly.
Else if no notices exist, Then 'No notices' message is shown.
4.If a new notice is posted since the employee last visit, Then it is highlighted as new.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Notice Board card shows notices with titles, category tags, and dates
 2. Verify that clicking a notice shows full content
 3. Verify that notices are sorted by most recent first
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that Notice Board card with no notices shows empty state
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that category tags are color-coded
 
 Edge Cases:
 6. Verify that a notice with very long content (2000+ chars) displays properly
 7. Verify that 100+ notices load with pagination or scroll
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that Notice Board loads within 2 seconds

User Story:
As an Employee, I should be able to view weekly timecard summary showing
 - Total hours, Regular hours, Overtime, Break time
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to view a summary of their weekly timecard on the dashboard showing total hours worked, regular hours, overtime hours, and total break time. This gives a quick snapshot of the current week's work hours for the employee to track against expected hours.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the employee views the Weekly Timecard Summary, Then Total Hours, Regular Hours, Overtime, and Break Time are displayed.
 2. If overtime exists, Then it is highlighted separately from regular hours.
 3. If no hours recorded this week, Then all values show 0.
 4. If the employee clocks out, Then the summary updates to reflect the new total for the week.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Weekly Timecard Summary shows Total Hours, Regular Hours, Overtime, Break Time
 2. Verify that Total Hours = Regular + Overtime
 3. Verify that overtime is calculated correctly based on weekly threshold (e.g., >40 hours)
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that a new employee with no timecards shows 0 for all values
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that overtime hours are highlighted with a distinct color/label
 6. Verify that the card updates in real-time after clock out
 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that break time is accurately subtracted from total work hours
 8. Verify that the summary recalculates when a timecard is edited by HR Admin
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that the summary card calculates and displays within 1 second

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to view weekly tasks by day with task details.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to view their tasks organized by day for the current week. Each day section shows tasks with title, priority, status, and assignment details. This provides a weekly overview of workload distribution and helps with planning.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the employee views Weekly Tasks, Then tasks are organized by day.
 2. If a day has no tasks, Then that day shows 'No tasks'.
 3. If tasks span multiple days, Then they appear under each applicable day.
 4. If a task is clicked from the weekly view, Then its full details are shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Weekly Tasks shows tasks organized by day
 2. Verify that each day section shows task title, priority, and status
 3. Verify that clicking a task shows its full details
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that a day with no tasks shows 'No tasks' message
 5. Verify that a week with no tasks shows empty state
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that the current day is highlighted
 7. Verify that task priority uses color coding
 
 Edge Cases:
 8. Verify that a day with 20+ tasks displays all with scrolling
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that Weekly Tasks loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to view all timecards history
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to view their complete timecard history in the Timecard section of the Employee Portal sidebar. The history shows all past timecards with pay period, total hours, status (Approved/Pending/Rejected), and submission date. This allows employees to track their work hours over time and verify payroll accuracy.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the employee opens Timecard section, Then all timecards are listed chronologically.
 2. If a timecard is clicked, Then the daily breakdown is shown.
 3. If a timecard has been rejected, Then the rejection reason is visible in the detail view.
 4. If no timecards exist, Then an empty state message is shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Timecard section shows all past timecards
 2. Verify that each entry shows pay period, total hours, status, submission date
 3. Verify that clicking a timecard shows daily hour breakdown
 4. Verify that timecards are sorted newest first
 
 Negative:
 5. Verify that a new employee with no timecards shows 'No timecards yet' message
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that status uses colors (Approved=green, Pending=orange, Rejected=red)
 7. Verify that the Timecard icon is highlighted in sidebar
 
 Edge Cases:
 8. Verify that 52+ weekly timecards (full year) load with pagination
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that timecard history loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to send timecard to supervisor for approval
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to submit their completed timecard to their supervisor for review and approval. The employee reviews their hours for the period and clicks a Submit/Send button. Once submitted, the timecard status changes to 'Pending' and the supervisor is notified.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the employee clicks 'Send to Supervisor', Then the timecard is submitted to the supervisor.
 2. After submission, Then the status changes to 'Pending' and the supervisor is notified.
 3. If already submitted, Then the Submit button is disabled.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking 'Send to supervisor' submits the timecard
 2. Verify that the timecard status changes to 'Pending' after submission
 3. Verify that the supervisor receives a notification
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that submitting a timecard with 0 hours shows a warning
 5. Verify that submitting an already-submitted timecard is blocked
 6. Verify that submitting a timecard for a future period is blocked
 
 UI Scenarios:
 7. Verify that after submission the button changes to 'Submitted' (disabled)
 
 Edge Cases:
 9. Verify that double-clicking Submit does not send duplicate submissions
 
 Security:
 10. Verify that the employee cannot modify the timecard after submission
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that timecard submission completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to submit overtime request.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to submit an overtime request when they need to work beyond their scheduled hours. The form captures the date, expected overtime hours, reason for overtime, and supervisor for approval. This ensures overtime is pre-approved and tracked for payroll.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When 'Submit Overtime Request' is clicked, Then the overtime form opens.
 2. If valid details are entered and submitted, Then the request goes to the supervisor.
 3. After submission, Then the request status shows 'Pending'.
 4. If the supervisor approves, Then the status changes to 'Approved' and the overtime hours reflect in the timecard.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that overtime request form shows date, hours, reason, and supervisor fields
 2. Verify that submitting a valid request (date: '2026-10-15', hours: '3', reason: 'Patient emergency') works
 3. Verify that the request appears in the supervisor's queue
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that submitting without a reason shows required field error
 5. Verify that requesting negative hours shows validation error
 6. Verify that requesting overtime for a past date shows warning
 
 UI Scenarios:
 7. Verify that the overtime request form is accessible from Timecard section
 
 Edge Cases:
 8. Verify that requesting overtime when already at maximum allowed hours shows a limit warning
 
 Security:
 9. Verify that overtime requests are logged with submission timestamp
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that overtime request submission completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to view all, approved, pending and rejected timecard.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to filter their timesheets by status using tabs: All, Approved, Pending, and Rejected. This helps employees quickly find timesheets that need attention or verify approved ones.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If 'All' tab is selected, Then all timecards display.
  Else if 'Approved', Then only approved.
  Else if 'Pending', Then only pending.
  Else if 'Rejected', Then only rejected with reasons.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that 'All' tab shows all timesheets
 2. Verify that 'Approved' tab shows only approved timesheets
 3. Verify that 'Pending' tab shows only pending timesheets
 4. Verify that 'Rejected' tab shows rejected timesheets with rejection reasons
 
 Negative:
 5. Verify that a tab with no matching timesheets shows empty state
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that four tabs (All, Approved, Pending, Rejected) are displayed
 7. Verify that the active tab is highlighted
 8. Verify that each tab shows a count of matching timesheets
 
 Edge Cases:
 9. Verify that switching tabs rapidly doesn't cause display issues
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that switching tabs completes within 1 second

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to submit a leave request.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to submit a leave request from the Leaves section in the sidebar. The Figma shows the Leave Management page with tabs for 'Leaves Management' and 'Leave Calendar'. The request form captures leave type, from-to dates, reason, and any supporting documents. Once submitted, the request goes through dual approval (Supervisor then HR).
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the employee clicks 'Request Leave', Then the form opens with leave type, dates, reason fields.
 2. If all fields are filled and submitted, Then the request goes to the supervisor for approval.
 3. After submission, Then the request shows Supervisor: 'Pending', HR: 'Awaiting'.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking 'Request Leave' opens the leave request form
 2. Verify that selecting leave type 'PTO', dates ranges , reason and submitting works
 3. Verify that the submitted request shows dual approval status (Supervisor: Pending, HR: Awaiting)
 4. Verify that the supervisor receives a notification
 
 Negative:
 5. Verify that submitting without selecting leave type shows error
 6. Verify that submitting without entering dates shows error
 7. Verify that requesting leave for past dates shows warning
 8. Verify that requesting leave exceeding remaining balance shows quota warning
 
 UI Scenarios:
 9. Verify that the Leaves icon is highlighted in sidebar when active
 10. Verify that leave type shows as dropdown (PTO, Sick Leave, Annual Leave, Emergency Leave)
 11. Verify that date range picker shows from-to selection
 
 Edge Cases:
 12. Verify that requesting leave for a single day works (from = to)
 13. Verify that requesting leave overlapping with assigned shifts shows conflict warning
 14. Verify that submitting multiple leave requests for overlapping dates is prevented
 
 Security:
 15. Verify that the employee can only submit leave for themselves
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that leave request submission completes within 2 seconds

User Story:
As an Employee, I should be able to view leave balance cards showing
 - Annual Leave, Sick Leave, PTO, Emergency Leave balances
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to view their leave balance cards showing remaining days for each leave type. The cards display: Annual Leave , Sick Leave ), Personaland Unpaid leaves . This helps employees plan their time off knowing exactly how many days they have available.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the employee opens Leaves, Then balance cards for all leave types are displayed.
 2. If no leave has been used, Then the full allocation is shown as remaining.
 3. If a leave request is approved or cancelled, Then the corresponding balance card updates to reflect the change.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that leave balance cards show Annual Leave, Sick Leave, Personal, Unpaid Leave
 2. Verify that each card shows total, used, and remaining days
 3. Verify that remaining = total - used for each type
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that a balance of 0 remaining is shown when all leave is used
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that balance cards are displayed at the top of the Leaves page
 6. Verify that cards use color coding (e.g., low balance in red)
 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that balance updates immediately after a leave request is approved
 8. Verify that balance recalculates if an approved leave is cancelled
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that leave balance cards load within 1 second

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to view past leave requests in different tabs.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to view their past leave requests filtered by status tabs. The Figma Leave Management screen shows a list of requests with employee name, leave type badge (PTO/Sick/Annual/Emergency), date range, reason, and approval status. Tabs allow filtering to see all, approved, pending, or rejected requests.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the employee views past requests, Then all requests display with type, dates, reason, status.
 2. If filtered by status tab, Then only matching requests show.
 3. If the 'Rejected' tab is selected, Then rejected requests are shown with the rejection reason visible.
 4. If a tab has no matching requests, Then an empty state message is shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that past leave requests list shows leave type, date range, reason, and status
 2. Verify that filtering by 'Approved' shows only approved requests
 3. Verify that filtering by 'Rejected' shows rejected requests with reasons
 4. Verify that filtering by 'Pending' shows only pending requests 
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that a tab with no matching requests shows empty state
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that leave type badges are color-coded
 6. Verify that status tabs are visible above the request list
 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that 100+ past requests load with pagination
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that leave request list loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to cancel a pending leave request.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to cancel a leave request that is still in 'Pending' status (not yet approved). A Cancel button is available on pending requests. Once cancelled, the request is removed from the approval queue and any reserved leave days are returned to the balance.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the employee clicks 'Cancel' on a pending request, Then a confirmation dialog is shown.
 2. If the employee confirms cancellation, Then the request is cancelled and removed from the approval queue.
 3. After cancellation, Then reserved leave days are restored to the balance.
 4. If the request is already approved, Then Cancel is not available (must contact HR).
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking 'Cancel' on a pending request cancels it
 2. Verify that the cancelled request is removed from the approval queue
 3. Verify that leave balance is restored after cancellation
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that Cancel is not available on approved requests
 5. Verify that Cancel is not available on rejected requests
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that Cancel button is visible only on pending requests
 7. Verify that a confirmation dialog appears before cancellation
 
 Edge Cases:
 8. Verify that cancelling a request while the supervisor is reviewing it is handled gracefully
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that cancellation completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to view my assigned shift schedule.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to view their assigned shift schedule in My Shifts section. The schedule shows upcoming and past shifts with shift type, time range, and status.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1.When the employee navigates to the My Shifts tab, then the shift schedule screen is displayed.
2.When the screen loads, then the current week’s schedule is shown by default.
3.When the schedule is displayed, then each day shows the assigned shift name and timing.
4.If no shift is assigned for a day, then it is marked as “OFF”.
5.If the employee navigates between weeks or selects a different month, then the schedule updates accordingly.
6.When viewing Past Requests, then each record shows change details and status.
7.When a status filter (All, Approved, Pending, Rejected) is selected, then the list updates accordingly.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that My Shifts shows assigned shifts with type, time range, status
 2. Verify that upcoming shifts are highlighted differently from past shifts
 
 Negative:
 3. Verify that no assigned shifts shows 'No shifts scheduled'
 
 UI Scenarios:
 4. Verify that shifts are color-coded by type
 
 Edge Cases:
 5. Verify that a month with 30+ shift entries loads properly
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that My Shifts loads within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to submit shift change request
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to submit a request to change their assigned shift. The Figma Shift Change Request screen shows entries with employee name, date, from-shift, to-shift, reason, and status (Pending/Approved/Rejected). The employee specifies which shift they want to change to and the reason. This goes through supervisor approval.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the employee clicks 'Request Shift Change', Then the form opens.
 2. If valid details are entered, Then the request is submitted with 'Pending' status.
 3. After submission, Then the supervisor receives a notification.
 4. If the supervisor approves the request, Then the employee's schedule updates with the new shift.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking 'Request Shift Change' opens the request form
 2. Verify that specifying from one shift to other and reason submits successfully
 3. Verify that the request shows in the Shift Change Request list with 'Pending' status
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that submitting without a reason shows error
 5. Verify that requesting a change to the same shift shows warning
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that the Shift Change Request page shows From shift, To shift,Start and End Date, Reason columns
 7. Verify that 'Pending' status shows in orange badge
 
 Edge Cases:
 8. Verify that submitting a change request for a past shift date is blocked
 9. Verify that submitting multiple requests for the same date is handled
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that shift change request submission completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to view shift change request status in different tabs.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to view their shift change requests filtered by status tabs: All, Pending, Approved, Rejected. The Figma shows tab navigation at the top of the Shift Change Request page with each tab filtering the request list.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If 'All' tab selected(default), Then all requests show.
  Else if 'Pending', Then only pending.
  Else if 'Approved', Then only approved with approval details.
  Else if 'Rejected', Then only rejected with rejection reason.
 2. If a tab has no matching requests, Then an empty state message is displayed.
 3. After switching tabs, Then the list updates immediately to show only matching entries.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that 'All' tab shows all shift change requests
 2. Verify that 'Pending' tab shows only pending requests
 3. Verify that 'Approved' tab shows only approved requests with approval details
 4. Verify that 'Rejected' tab shows rejected requests with rejection reason
 
 Negative:
 5. Verify that a tab with no matching requests shows empty state
 
 UI Scenarios:
 6. Verify that tabs show: All, Pending, Approved, Rejected
 7. Verify that active tab is highlighted
 
 Edge Cases:
 8. Verify that switching tabs rapidly doesn't cause display issues
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that switching tabs completes within 1 second

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to accept / decline shift swap request.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to accept or decline a shift swap request from another employee. When a colleague requests to swap shifts, the employee receives a notification and can review the swap details (their shift, proposed swap shift, requestor name) and accept or decline.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When a swap request is received, Then the employee sees swap details.
 2. If 'Accept' is clicked, Then shifts are swapped and both parties notified.
 3. If 'Decline' is clicked, Then the swap is rejected and the requestor notified.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that shift swap request shows requestor name, their shift, and my shift details
 2. Verify that clicking 'Accept' swaps the shifts for both employees
 3. Verify that clicking 'Decline' rejects the swap
 4. Verify that both employees receive notifications after accept/decline
 
 Negative:
 5. Verify that accepting a swap for a past date is blocked
 6. Verify that accepting when the target shift conflicts with existing leave is blocked
 
 UI Scenarios:
 7. Verify that Accept and Decline buttons are visible on the swap request
 8. Verify that swap details clearly show 'Your Shift' vs 'Proposed Shift'
 
 Edge Cases:
 9. Verify that if the swap is accepted but the original shift was already changed by HR, a conflict message appears
 
 Security:
 10. Verify that an employee cannot accept a swap on behalf of another employee
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that shift swap acceptance completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to upload new files and create new folders.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to manage their documents in the Documents section of the Employee Portal. The employee can upload new files (certifications, IDs, personal documents) and create folders to organize them. This self-service capability reduces HR workload for document management.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When Upload is clicked, Then file dialog opens.
 2. If a valid file is selected, Then it uploads to the current folder.
 3. When 'New Folder' is clicked and named, Then the folder is created and appears in the folder tree.
 4. If a file is successfully uploaded, Then it is immediately visible in the current folder's document list.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking Upload opens file selection dialog
 2. Verify that uploading valid document adds it to the documents list
 3. Verify that creating a folder adds it to the folder tree
 4. Verify that uploading a file into a specific folder stores it there
 
 Negative:
 5. Verify that uploading a file >25MB shows size error
 6. Verify that creating a folder with duplicate name shows warning
 7. Verify that uploading unsupported file types is rejected
 
 UI Scenarios:
 8. Verify that Documents icon is highlighted in sidebar
 9. Verify that folders show folder icon and files show file type icon
 
 Edge Cases:
 10. Verify that creating nested folders works
 11. Verify that uploading 20 files simultaneously is handled
 
 Security:
 12. Verify that uploaded files are scanned for malware
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that file upload of 10MB completes within 5 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to view and sign compliance documents.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to view compliance documents assigned by HR and apply digital signatures. Documents like HIPAA acknowledgment, safety protocols, and policy agreements require employee signature for compliance tracking.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When documents requiring signature are assigned, Then they appear with a 'Sign' indicator.
 2. If the employee clicks Sign, Then the digital signature is applied.
 3. After signing, Then the document status changes to 'Signed' and the signed date is recorded.
 4. If all compliance documents are signed, Then no pending signature indicators are shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that compliance documents show 'Sign Required' indicator
 2. Verify that clicking Sign applies the digital signature
 3. Verify that the document status changes to 'Signed' after signature
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that signing an already-signed document shows 'Already signed'
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that unsigned documents are visually distinguished from signed ones
 
 Edge Cases:
 6. Verify that signing while offline queues the signature for when connection returns
 
 Security:
 7. Verify that digital signatures are cryptographically secured
 8. Verify that signed documents cannot be modified after signature
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that document signing completes within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to view documents organized in folders
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to browse their documents organized in a folder hierarchy. Folders group documents by category (e.g., Certifications, Personal, Compliance) for easy navigation.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When Documents is opened, Then folders and files are displayed.
 2. If a folder is clicked, Then its contents are shown.
 3. If the breadcrumb is clicked, Then navigation returns to the selected parent level.
 4. If a folder is empty, Then an empty state message is shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that Documents page shows folder structure
 2. Verify that clicking a folder opens it and shows contained documents
 3. Verify that navigating back from a folder returns to the parent view
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that an empty folder shows 'No documents' message
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that folders show folder icon and document count
 6. Verify that breadcrumb navigation shows the current folder path
 
 Edge Cases:
 7. Verify that deeply nested folders are navigable
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that folder contents load within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to download my employment documents.
Description of User Story:
This story enables an employee to view and download their employment-related documents from the system. Employees can access documents such as offer letters, contracts, payslips, and other official files.

Each document is listed with basic details like document name and date, and includes a download option. By clicking the download button, the employee can save the document to their device.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When Download is clicked on a document, Then the file is saved to the device.
 2. If the download completes successfully, Then the downloaded file matches the original document stored in the system.
 3. If Download is clicked on a document inside a folder, Then the correct file is retrieved and downloaded.
 4. If the document is no longer available, Then an error message is shown.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that clicking Download saves the file to the device
 2. Verify that the downloaded file matches the original
 
 Negative:
 3. Verify that downloading a deleted document shows error
 
 UI Scenarios:
 4. Verify that Download icon/button is visible on each document row
 
 Edge Cases:
 5. Verify that downloading a large file (50MB) completes without timeout
 
 Security:
 6. Verify that documents are downloaded over HTTPS
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that document download initiates within 2 seconds

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to update task status.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to update the status of their assigned tasks (e.g., from 'Pending' to 'In Progress' to 'Completed'). This tracks task progression and keeps supervisors informed.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. If the employee changes task status to 'In Progress', Then the task reflects the new status.
 2. If changed to 'Completed', Then the task is marked as done and the pending count decreases.
 3. If the employee tries to revert a completed task to 'Pending', Then a confirmation prompt is shown before the change is applied.
 4. After any status change, Then the supervisor can see the updated status in real time.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that changing status from 'Pending' to 'In Progress' saves correctly
 2. Verify that changing to 'Completed' marks the task as done
 3. Verify that the supervisor can see the updated status
 
 Negative:
 4. Verify that changing status of a completed task back to 'Pending' shows warning
 
 UI Scenarios:
 5. Verify that status dropdown shows: Pending, In Progress, Completed
 
 Edge Cases:
 6. Verify that updating status while another user views the task shows real-time update
Performance Scenarios: 1. Verify that status update saves within 1 second

User Story: As an Employee, I should be able to log notes against a task.
Description of User Story: This story enables an employee to add notes to a task for documentation, progress updates, or issue reporting. Each note shows timestamp and author.
Acceptance Criteria (If/Then/Else/Then):
1. When the employee adds a note to a task, Then it saves with timestamp and author.
 2. If multiple notes exist on a task, Then they are displayed in chronological order.
 3. If another team member also added notes, Then all notes from all contributors are visible with their author names.
 4. After adding a note, Then it immediately appears in the notes history without requiring a page refresh.
Test Scenarios:
Positive Functional:
 1. Verify that adding a note (e.g., 'Completed medication round, no issues') saves with timestamp
 2. Verify that multiple notes can be added to the same task
